title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/44/L.5 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: PBI pertains to convening an international conference of plenipotentiaries in Vienna from 8 to 26 April 1991 to consider the draft convention on liability of operators of transport terminals in international trade (per draft resolution A/C.6/44/L.5). Conference-servicing requirements are estimated on a full-cost basis at $1,635,200 (pre-session documentation $197,800; meeting services/interpretation $289,500; in-session documentation $330,700; post-session documentation $26,300; summary records for 45 meetings $790,900). However, based on the 1990-1991 proposed programme budget (section 29, conference services) provision for temporary assistance for meetings, it is estimated that no additional cost would be incurred under section 29—i.e., these servicing costs would be absorbed within existing resources. The only additional cost not provided for is $20,200 for travel and subsistence: $3,100 for the Legal Counsel and $17,100 for representatives. No provision exists for this in the 1990-1991 proposed budget; financing would come from the contingency fund per resolution 41/213, or, if unavailable, through redeployment by reducing International Trade Law Branch staff attendance at working/study groups from 12 to 8 weeks. The conference would require interpretation in six official languages and would be entitled to summary records per GA resolution 37/14 C.

Conference-servicing requirements for the 1991 Vienna plenipotentiary conference were estimated at $1,635,200 at full cost (pre-session docs $197,800; meeting servicing $289,300; in-session docs $330,700; post-session docs $26,300; summary records $190,900), but the Secretary-General indicates these would be absorbed within Section 29 temporary assistance for meetings with no additional appropriation expected. The only additional resources requested are $20,200 for travel and subsistence of the Legal Counsel ($3,100) and representatives ($17,100), to be financed from the contingency fund for 1990-1991; if not available, resources would be redeployed by reducing attendance at working/study groups from 12 to 8 weeks.

The Secretary-General estimates full conference-servicing costs for the biennium 1990-1991 at $621,700 ($67,700 for the Special High-level Council and $554,000 for the Scientific and Technical Committee), but states these can be absorbed within existing permanent conference-servicing capacity under section 29; therefore no additional regular budget resources are requested for 1990-1991. Travel and DSA for meetings total $602,200 for the biennium and are anticipated to be funded from extrabudgetary resources. A small Decade secretariat at UNOG is to be funded entirely from extrabudgetary resources and will prepare the requested progress and biennial reports, requiring no additional regular budget resources. The High-level Council (6 members) meets annually in Geneva; the Scientific and Technical Committee (20-25 experts) meets three times per year in 1990-1991, then annually thereafter.

stages.notes: SG states costs of implementing the Convention should be met from the regular budget. For 1991, non-conference items total $206,000 to be absorbed within Section 23’s provision for potential mandates (no additional appropriation or contingency fund). Breakdown: travel and subsistence for 10 Committee members for a 3‑week session ($55,000); emoluments guided by GA res. 35/218—$5,000 for Chair and $3,000 for each of 9 members ($32,000); general temporary assistance for the Centre for Human Rights ($119,000) equivalent to 12 work‑months at Professional level (6 at P‑3, 6 at P‑2) and 6 work‑months at General Service. Conference‑servicing estimated at $968,000 (Meeting of States parties $97,600; Committee on the Rights of the Child $870,400) but expected to be covered within Section 29 permanent capacity—no additional appropriation under Section 29. Additional appropriation of $22,700 under Section 31 (staff assessment) is fully offset by income. Activities begin upon entry into force in 1991; first meeting of States parties (1 day, HQ) and first Committee session (3 weeks, Geneva). Committee meets annually; emoluments are annual; long‑term staffing needs to be determined later.

title: Measures to improve the situation and ensure the human rights of all migrant workers : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/44/L.74 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Secretary-General states that adoption of draft resolution A/C.3/44/L.74 would entail no additional appropriations. The two-week 1990 meeting of the Working Group is already provided for in the draft calendar of conferences and existing programme resources (subprogramme 4, programme element 4.1). Existing resources include $208,800 under general temporary assistance in Section 23 for professional and secretarial support during peak periods; this would cover the technical revision work without new funds. Holding the Working Group in New York would be an exception to GA resolution 40/243 (the established headquarters for this body is Geneva). An additional output (preparation of the technical revision of the draft convention text) would be added under programme element 4.1, but with no extra cost.

Secretary-General states no additional appropriations are required to: (a) technically revise the draft convention text and transmit results to Governments, and (b) hold a two-week meeting of the Working Group in 1990. Resources for general temporary assistance totaling $208,800 are already included in the proposed programme budget 1990-1991 (section 23, para. 23.37) and can cover the technical revision. The Working Group’s two 1990 sessions and related conference servicing are already in the draft 1990 conference calendar. One additional output will be added under programme element 4.1: preparation of technical revisions of the draft convention. Holding the Working Group meeting in New York would be an exception to GA resolution 40/243 (the Working Group’s established headquarters is Geneva).

title: Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/44/L.29 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Pre-sessional working group of 5 members to meet up to 5 working days prior to the ninth session of CEDAW (22 Jan–2 Feb 1990, New York). One-time DSA for 5 members and 3 staff totals $14,200; the Secretary-General indicates these additional requirements can be absorbed within $280,800 already proposed for the Committee in the 1990–1991 budget. Conference servicing (interpretation in all six languages) to be met from Section 29, Conference and library services; no extra documentation required. Existing provisions include $253,800 for travel and subsistence for 23 Committee members and $27,000 for 6 staff (including one human rights officer from Geneva). Conclusion: no additional appropriations required.
stages.budget_section: Section 8, Activities on global social development issues

title: Second Industrial Development Decade for Africa (1991-2000) : Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/44/L.74 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Full cost to the United Nations for preparatory activities totals $461,900, of which $280,000 is anticipated to be absorbed under Section 24 (regular programme of technical cooperation). The net additional amount requested under Section 13 is $181,900 to finance activities (ii) staff missions, (v) two expert group meetings, and (vi) consultations with UN agencies and financial institutions. UNIDO’s related costs are $883,900 (including 73 consultant work-months) for a total combined cost of $1,345,800. No posts are created. If contingency fund resources are insufficient, activities may be postponed per GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211. The $181,900 figure is presented for the 1990-1991 biennium; no per-year split is provided.
stages.budget_section: Section 13, Economic Commission for Africa (industrial development)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/44/L.43, A/44/L.44 and A/44/L.45 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: This SG statement (A/C.5/44/43) provides programme budget implications for draft resolutions A/44/L.43, A/44/L.44 and A/44/L.45 (Question of Palestine) for the biennium 1990-1991. The SG estimates full-cost conference-servicing requirements of $3,482,300 across 1990-1991 (Annex V) and substantive servicing for a combined Latin American and Caribbean seminar and NGO symposium in Buenos Aires (5–9 Feb 1990) of $114,600 (para. 12; Annex IV). However, the SG concludes that no additional appropriations are required under Sections 1, 27 or 29 because temporary assistance for meetings under Section 29 was budgeted to accommodate additional meetings consistent with prior years (para. 14), and the Committee proposes to implement its 1990 combined seminar/symposium economically to avoid any additional appropriations (para. 15). Activities span multiple meetings over 1990-1991 (regional seminars, NGO symposia, preparatory meetings, international NGO meetings) with multilingual documentation and interpretation; some meetings may require exceptions to GA resolution 40/243 when held away from established headquarters and may necessitate planning missions and rental of premises and equipment (Annex I–II). No new posts are proposed.
stages.budget_section: Section 1 (Overall policy-making, direction and co-ordination); Section 27 (Public information); Section 29 (Conference services – temporary assistance for meetings)

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decided, without a vote, to inform the General Assembly that, should it adopt draft resolutions A/44/L.43, A/44/L.44 and A/44/L.45 (Question of Palestine), no additional appropriations would be required under sections 1, 27 or 29 of the proposed programme budget for the biennium 1990-1991. ACABQ and Committee on Conferences recommendations were presented orally; no amounts or posts specified.
stages.budget_section: Sections 1 (Overall policy-making, direction and co-ordination), 27 (Public information), and 29 (Conference and library services)

title: United Nations Conference on Environment and Development : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/44/L.86 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: The Secretary-General estimates conference-servicing costs for the March 1990 organizational session of the Preparatory Committee at $141,500 (two weeks; 42 pages pre-session, 10 pages in-session, 10 pages post-session; six official languages). However, based on the provision for temporary assistance for meetings under Section 29 of the proposed programme budget for 1990-1991, these costs are expected to be absorbed; therefore, no additional appropriations are required at this stage. Additional requirements for 1990-1991 will be submitted to a resumed session of the General Assembly after the organizational session defines the preparatory process; requirements for 1992 will be addressed in the 1992-1993 budget outline. The resolution also establishes a voluntary fund to support developing countries’ participation (extrabudgetary) and calls for an ad hoc secretariat (Geneva, with units in New York and Nairobi) headed by a Secretary-General of the Conference; no staffing counts or costs are specified in this statement.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/44/L.67 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.
Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/45/L.2 : report of the 5th Committee, General Assembly, 45th session.

stages.notes: Non-recurrent appropriation requested for ONUVEH (United Nations Observer Group for the Verification of Elections in Haiti), operating from early Oct 1990 to Jan 1991. Total estimated requirements for 1990-1991 are $9,368,300, primarily in 1990. Additional staff assessment of $353,300 under section 31 is fully offset by income section 1 and is not new money. Core mission staffing comprises 87 international staff (50 Professional and above; 18 Field Service officers; 10 Field Service radio technicians for 3–4 weeks; 9 General Service). Local staff: 112 local-level staff as general temporary assistance (budget line $198,000). Security experts: 150 provided by Member States (65 in October; 85 on stand-by, expected to remain 45 days). Election monitors: 80 during election periods (40 UN staff and 40 provided by Governments at no cost other than travel and DSA), serving up to 10 days; supplemented by up to 100 UN agency staff on loan (~8 days each). Public information campaign in Haiti: $200,000. Use of surplus communications equipment from UNTAG; equipment to be returned to stockpile or redeployed, with residual value credited to the regular budget. Secretary-General considers these extraordinary expenses should be treated outside the contingency fund per para. 11 of annex I to GA res. 41/213.
stages.posts_levels: ASG (Special Representative), D-2 (Chief electoral officer), Professional and above, Field Service (incl. radio technicians), General Service, Local-level support (separately budgeted as GTA)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/45/L.3 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/45/L.3 would extend the provision for the Secretary-General’s Personal Representative and supporting staff and related costs through 1991 for OSGAP (Office of the Secretary-General in Afghanistan and Pakistan). The Secretary-General indicates the additional requirement is of an extraordinary nature related to peace and security and should be treated outside the contingency fund. 1990 figures (context only): initial appropriation $5,855,100; revised estimated requirements $6,354,500; ACABQ concurred to commitment authority up to $1,482,400 under GA resolution 44/203 (unforeseen and extraordinary expenses). 1991 detailed estimates include: consultants $53,000; rental and operation of aircraft $2,459,600 (assumes 200 flight hours); rental/maintenance of premises $280,700 (Islamabad $50,600, Peshawar $13,300, Kabul $148,800; plus maintenance $68,000; Kabul rent-free status uncertain); miscellaneous services include $106,800 for contractual security. Staffing context for 1991: continuation of posts authorized for 1990 (1 ASG, 1 D-1, 3 P-5, 1 P-4, 6 GS (Other), 8 Field Service, 13 Local), plus those added from 15 March 1990 with ACABQ concurrence (1 GS (Other), 3 Field Service, 21 Local), and five additional proposed posts in 1991 (2 P-4 and 3 Local); total staffing in 1991 would be 63 temporary posts across mission area and HQ. The additional appropriation requested for 1991 is explicitly non-recurrent.
stages.budget_section: Section 1 of the programme budget for the biennium 1990-1991

stages.notes: ACABQ reviewed SG’s estimate for draft resolution A/45/L.3 (Afghanistan/OSGAP). SG estimated a non-recurrent requirement of $6,742,100 under section 1 for 1991, plus staff assessment of $379,200 (offset). ACABQ recommends reducing section 1 to $6,150,000 for 1991 and $336,100 for staff assessment (offset) and specifies financing should be without recourse to the contingency fund. ACABQ questions the need for the 5 proposed temporary posts (2 P-4, 3 Local) and urges maximum economy, including savings on aircraft rental. ACABQ expects host Government support for local staff, security, vehicles and premises. Continuation through 1991 of existing posts noted: 33 posts authorized for 1990 (1 ASG, 1 D-1, 3 P-5, 1 P-4, 6 GS (other level), 8 Field Service, 13 Local) plus 25 posts added from 15 March 1990 (1 GS (other level), 3 Field Service, 21 Local) — total 58 posts, in addition to 10 military adviser posts. Staff assessment is fully offset by income and not additional programmatic funding.
stages.posts_levels: P-4, P-4, Local level, Local level, Local level

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/45/L.10 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Two sessions of the Ad Hoc Committee on the Indian Ocean in New York during 1991 (one week in April; two weeks in July). Full conference-servicing cost estimated at $634,400 (first session: $225,200; second session: $409,200), including interpretation, translation of pre-, in-, and post-session documentation, and summary records in six languages. Despite the full cost estimate, the Secretary-General indicates no additional appropriations are required under sections 2A or 29 for 1990-1991, as requirements are expected to be absorbed within existing permanent and temporary conference-servicing resources. Costs related to convening the 1992 Conference would be proposed under the 1992-1993 budget.
stages.budget_section: Section 29, Conference servicing (with substantive support under Section 2A: Political and Security Council affairs; peace-keeping activities)

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.1/45/L.10 requests two preparatory sessions of the Ad Hoc Committee on the Indian Ocean in 1991 (one week in April; two weeks in July) and continued assistance including provision of summary records. Full cost estimate for conference servicing in New York totals $634,400 (first session $225,200; second session $409,200), comprising interpretation in six GA languages, documentation (pre-session 10 pages/3 docs; in-session 15 pages/5 docs; post-session 10 pages/3 docs) issued in six languages, and summary records (10 meetings for first session; 20 meetings for second). However, based on temporary assistance for meetings capacity under section 29 and the biennial conference calendar, the Secretary-General estimates that no additional appropriations are required under sections 2A or 29 for 1990-1991; costs would be absorbed. Substantive support for the Ad Hoc Committee is already provided under section 2A. Any provisions for convening the Conference on the Indian Ocean in 1992 would be addressed in the proposed programme budget for 1992-1993.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/45/L.3 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG estimates no additional resources are required under sections 26 or 29 for implementing draft resolution A/C.6/45/L.3; costs are expected to be absorbed within the 1990-1991 programme budget. Completion of the draft handbook on peaceful settlement of disputes entails no additional resource requirements. Substantive servicing falls under Section 26, Legal activities; conference servicing under Section 29, Conference services.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/45/L.39 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG estimates total requirements of $32,600 (travel/DSA for eight experts: $28,800; travel/DSA for one DDA staff: $3,800). The $3,800 for staff travel/DSA can be absorbed within the existing appropriation for Disarmament Affairs; thus the additional new requirements are $28,800 for the experts. Activities relate to a five-day experts meeting at Addis Ababa in 1991; OAU to provide conference-servicing and support services. Funding to be sought from the contingency fund (per GA resolution 41/213); if contingency resources are insufficient, activities would be postponed (per GA resolution 42/211). Section numbering in the text shows OCR inconsistencies; the affected area is Disarmament Affairs.

stages.notes: One-time costs for a five-day meeting of eight experts in Addis Ababa (1991). Full cost estimated at $32,600, of which $3,800 (travel/DSA for one staff member of the Department for Disarmament Affairs) to be absorbed under section 2E; net additional appropriation requested is $28,800 under section 2B. OAU to provide conference-servicing and other support services. Implementation subject to availability of contingency fund resources; if not available, activity would be postponed per GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211.

title: Charting potential uses of resources allocated to military activities for civilian endeavours to protect the environment : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/45/L.49 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: PBI for draft resolution A/C.1/45/L.49. Additional requirements arise in 1991 under section 28 only; estimated at $144,400 for: (i) travel and DSA for six experts for three sessions; (ii) six work-months of consultancy services plus travel and DSA for two consultants attending three sessions; (iii) two work-months of temporary assistance for secretarial support. Conference-servicing requirements (section 29) are expected to be met within existing capacity; thus no additional resources under section 29 despite an annexed cost estimate for documentation. The Department for Disarmament Affairs indicates no capacity to redeploy resources; if costs cannot be met from the contingency fund, implementation would be postponed per GA guidelines.

stages.notes: SG proposes establishing a group of six experts in 1991 with three sessions. Substantive additional requirements under section 2B total $144,400 for 1991 (travel/DSA for six experts, six work-months of consultancy services including consultants’ travel/DSA, and two work-months of temporary assistance). Estimated conference-servicing costs of $85,000 are identified but no additional resources are required under section 29; these would be met within existing conference-servicing capacity. The Department for Disarmament Affairs cannot absorb the $144,400 within section 2B; financing would need to come from the contingency fund or the activity would be postponed. No posts are created.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/45/L.73 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.3/45/L.73 would convene a 5‑day Preparatory Committee session in Geneva in Sept 1991. The session’s conference‑servicing cost is estimated at $369,400 (assumptions: 5 days; documentation of 250 pre‑session pages, 60 in‑session pages, 32 post‑session pages; languages Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, Spanish). However, the Secretary‑General indicates that, based on existing provision for temporary assistance for meetings under Section 29 in the 1990‑1991 programme budget, no additional appropriations are required in 1990‑1991 (i.e., the 1991 session costs are to be absorbed). The resolution specifies funding through the regular budget without implications for programmes under Section 23. Additional requirements are anticipated in 1992‑1993 (strengthening the Centre for Human Rights with temporary staff, travel and consultants; and DPI public information), with an intention to include $1,500,000 in the proposed programme budget for 1992‑1993; first‑year (1992) breakdown is not provided in this document.

title: Confidence-building measures in outer space : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/45/L.22/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG statement on draft A/C.1/45/L.22/Rev.1 requests resources to establish a 12-member group of governmental experts to study confidence-building measures in outer space, with sessions: Geneva (1 week, Sep/Oct 1991), New York (1 week, Mar 1992), New York (2 weeks, Jul 1992), New York (2 weeks, Apr 1993). Substantive support includes 6 work-months of consultancy (2 in 1991, 3 in 1992, 1 in 1993), 4 work-months temporary secretarial assistance (1 in 1991, 2 in 1992, 1 in 1993), travel for one staff to Geneva, and travel/DSA for 12 experts. First-year (1991) additional requirement under Section 2B is $64,400; staff assessment of $1,400 (Section 31) is fully offset. Further estimated requirements: $166,900 for 1992 and $97,400 for 1993 (to be considered under the 1992-1993 budget). Conference-servicing full-cost estimate totals $295,700 (across four sessions), but per paragraph 14 no additional resources are expected under conference services (Section 29) as these would be met within permanent capacity. The Department for Disarmament Affairs indicates no capacity to absorb within Section 2B; funding would need to come from the contingency fund or be deferred if unavailable.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/45/L.44 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Additional requirements of $83,900 for 1991 to establish one Under-Secretary-General post under section 20. Staff assessment of $23,900 under section 31 is fully offset by income under income section 1. The Secretary-General indicates no resources could be redeployed from low-priority areas; if contingency fund resources are not available, establishment of the post would be deferred. Integration of the Division of Narcotic Drugs, INCB secretariat and UNFDAC into the United Nations International Drug Control Programme is to be implemented; UNFDAC resources placed under direct responsibility of the Programme head. Earlier SG plan envisioned an ASG post; draft resolution invites appointment at USG level, driving the additional cost.

SG states an additional appropriation of $83,900 is required in 1991 under section 20 to establish a post at the Under-Secretary-General level to head the new integrated United Nations International Drug Control Programme. If contingency fund resources are not available and no redeployment from low-priority areas can be identified, establishment of the post would be deferred. An additional $23,900 for staff assessment (section 31) is fully offset by income. The resolution restructures/merges existing entities (Division of Narcotic Drugs, INCB secretariat, UNFDAC) but no other regular budget costs are identified in this statement.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/45/L.20 and L.21 and Corr.1 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 45th session.

stages.notes: Covers draft resolutions A/45/L.20 and A/45/L.21 & Corr.1. For L.20 (Ad Hoc Committee of the Whole): conference-servicing full-cost estimates total $591,800 ($65,000 for the 2-day organizational session in April 1991; $526,800 for the 10-day regular session in September 1991), but the Secretary-General estimates no additional resources are required under Section 29 for 1990-1991 because temporary assistance for meetings was provisioned to absorb additional meetings; thus $0 new appropriation for Section 29. Holding the September 1991 session would require an exception to GA resolution 40/243 (para. 6). For L.21 & Corr.1 (commodity-related studies): existing provisions cover op. para. 4 (Section 5A) and op. para. 6 (Section 15/UNCTAD). Additional consultancy of $50,000 is required under Section 5A for op. paras. 5 (AfDB diversification fund feasibility) and 7 (capacity of African commodity sector, with UNIDO). Financing subject to the contingency fund under res. 41/213; if resources are not available and no redeployment is possible, preparation of the reports would be deferred per res. 42/211. No posts are created. One-time costs within the 1990-1991 biennium.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/45/L.31, A/45/L.33, A/45/L.38, A/45/L.39, A/45/L.40, A/45/L.41 and A/45/L.42 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG states that no additional appropriation is required under section 3 (Special Committee and related bodies) for 1991; activities for Intergovernmental Group ($80,000 full cost) and Special Committee regular work are covered by existing appropriations, using a 75% utilization ratio. Special projects allocation of $480,000 for the Special Committee in 1991 is to be financed from unspent balances within section 3A.3; grants to national liberation movements ($348,400) are covered by existing approved appropriations. The only additional appropriation requested is $117,000 under section 27 for 1991 DPI promotional activities (from a total need of $171,700 less $54,700 uncommitted within section 27). If contingency fund cannot finance the $117,000, the SG proposes deferring the biennial updating of 'Basic Facts about the United Nations' to 1992-1993.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/40/L.15 : report of the 5th Committee.

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decision: If the General Assembly adopts draft resolution A/40/L.15, no additional appropriations would be required at this stage. However, conference-servicing requirements would arise, estimated on a full-cost basis at $952,000. Any actual additional appropriations for conference servicing would be considered later in the session within the consolidated statement of conference-servicing requirements. The ACABQ report was presented orally; no quantitative recommendation is recorded here. The Secretary-General’s statement referenced was A/C.5/40/55 and Corr.1.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.37/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.37/Rev.1 endorses resolutions 1(V) and 2(V) of the Committee on the Development and Utilization of New and Renewable Sources of Energy. Breakdown of SG’s estimated additional resource requirements within the 1990-1991 biennium: (1) Resolution 1(V): $73,900 total—$10,000 under Section 5A (support to Nairobi Programme of Action) and $63,900 under Section 6—for consultancy services to prepare the requested studies/reports (total consultancy need is $80,000, with $6,100 already provided in Section 6; the $73,900 is the incremental requirement). (2) Resolution 2(V): $113,600 under Section 6—$47,600 for travel and DSA of 15 experts (five-day meeting at UNHQ in 1991), $36,000 for nine staff trips to regions to collect data, and $30,000 for consultants. Conference-servicing costs for the expert group are estimated at $199,900 (pre-, in-, and post-session documentation and interpretation), but are expected to be absorbed within the permanent conference-servicing capacity (Section 29); thus no additional resources are requested for conference servicing. Contingency fund procedure (GA resolution 41/213) applies; if resources are not available, activities may have to be postponed.

title: Strengthening of the Office of the United Nations Disaster Relief Co-ordinator : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.22 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.22 operative para 4 would raise UNDRO emergency grants ceiling to $1 million per year (maintaining $50,000 per country per disaster). The 1990-1991 programme budget already contains $720,000 for grants under Section 22 (UNDRO). The Secretary-General assumes the $400,000 annual increase is shared between the regular budget and voluntary sources per GA resolution 37/144, resulting in an additional regular budget requirement of $240,000 for 1991 under Section 22. Funding is subject to the contingency fund (GA resolution 41/213); UNDRO cannot absorb/redeploy $240,000 within Section 22, so if the contingency fund is insufficient, the $240,000 would not be made available. Operative para 2 on strengthening financial/manpower resources will be assessed and addressed in the proposed programme budget for 1992-1993; no posts or amounts are specified in this statement.

No amounts or posts are specified in this addendum; refer to A/C.3/45/L.37 for the detailed estimates for the 1990-1991 biennium. title: Implementation of the Programme of Action for the Least Developed Countries for the 1990s : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.57 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Addendum to the SG’s PBI for draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.57 (Second Committee). title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/45/L.16 and L.17 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 45th session.

stages.notes: SG states no additional resources are required for draft resolutions A/45/L.16 and A/45/L.17; activities are to be absorbed within existing 1990-1991 appropriations under Sections 3 and 27. Biennium appropriation under Section 3A.2 is $540,700. For 1991, the full-cost estimate for regular activities is $270,200; based on historical utilization (70%), estimated actual requirements are $189,000, which can be met from the unspent balance of $206,400 under Section 3A.2. Information activities are already programmed under Section 27 (Public information). Planned 1991 activities and full-cost estimates: 2 visiting missions ($73,500); 12 consultations with specialized agencies/international institutions ($106,200); 4 consultations with OAU ($50,500); 1 consultation with ECOSOC President ($7,100); participation in 10 NGO activities ($32,900); total $270,200. Appearances of representatives from Non-Self-Governing Territories are envisaged with no specific resources proposed; needs, if any, would be met within available resources. If the Special Committee holds meetings away from Headquarters in 1991, separate PBIs would be submitted later subject to conference services availability. Annex II shows historical utilization rates; 1990 special programme (30th anniversary) had separate estimated costs of $414,900 for the Special Committee and $211,100 for DPI (context only, not part of 1991 requirements).

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.38 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG statement on draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.38 for the Ad Hoc Committee session (1992) and a Conference (1993). Full-cost conference servicing estimates if held in New York: $283,500 for a 5-day Ad Hoc Committee session in 1992; $570,100 for a 10-day Conference in 1993; plus $10,300 for travel/subsistence of representatives of national liberation movements recognized by OAU. However, SG indicates these can be absorbed: no additional appropriation required under section 3 (substantive support) or section 32 (conference services) of the 1992-1993 proposed programme budget; the $10,300 travel can be absorbed within section 3 resources. Conference-servicing would draw on permanent capacity and provision for temporary assistance for meetings under section 32, subject to the final conferences calendar. If convened at Colombo, the host Government (Sri Lanka) would defray the location-related additional costs per GA resolution 40/243. Ad Hoc Committee entitled to summary records per GA 45/238 D; special conferences normally do not receive summary records per GA 37/14 C.
stages.budget_section: Section 3 (Political and Security Council affairs), subprogramme 2 (Services for political and security affairs)

However, these conference-servicing needs are expected to be absorbed within existing temporary assistance for meetings in the 1992-1993 biennium (no additional resources requested under section 32). Substantive servicing for the Ad Hoc Committee and the Conference is already provided under section 3 for 1992-1993; no additional requirements anticipated. One additional requirement identified: $10,300 in 1993 for travel/subsistence of representatives of OAU-recognized national liberation movements (to be financed from the contingency fund per GA res. 41/213). Host Government (Sri Lanka) is expected to defray additional costs of holding the Conference in Colombo rather than New York (per GA res. 40/243). Ad Hoc Committee summary records entitlement per GA res. 45/238 noted. This extraction reflects first-year (1992) additional resource request = $0; the $10,300 pertains to 1993.

title: Transparency in armaments : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.18/Rev.1, as orally amended : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Total estimated 1992 full-cost for three sessions of the panel is $644,700, comprising conference-servicing costs of $326,200 and non-conference-servicing costs of $318,500. Conference-servicing ($326,200) is anticipated to be absorbed under section 32 (no additional resources required under section 32). The additional appropriation requested under section 5 (Disarmament) is $318,500 for 1992 non-conference-servicing: travel and DSA for 18 experts ($69,709; $94,400; $94,400), seven work-months of consultancy services ($35,000), consultant travel/DSA ($3,800; $5,200; $5,200), and three work-months of temporary assistance (amount included in total). The Register is expected to become fully operational in 1993; initial 1993 costs are anticipated to be absorbed by the Department for Disarmament Affairs. A group of governmental experts will be convened in 1994; related PBIs to be considered in the 1994-1995 budget. Implementation is subject to the availability of the contingency fund under GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211; if not possible, activities may be postponed. Note: paragraph 15 contains a typographical discrepancy ($313,500), while paragraph 18 and the detailed breakdown confirm $318,500 as the additional requirement under section 5.
stages.budget_section: Section 5, Disarmament (subprogramme 3: Monitoring, analysis and studies)

stages.notes: SG statement (rule 153) for First Committee draft A/C.1/46/L.18/Rev.1. New money requested only under Section 5 for 1992: $205,300 (non-conference costs: travel/DSA for 18 experts; five work-months of consultancy incl. travel/DSA; two work-months temporary assistance). Conference-servicing costs for the two sessions total $227,500 (72,400 + 155,100) but are expected to be absorbed within Section 32 permanent capacity; therefore no additional resources under Section 32. The Register of Conventional Arms is anticipated to become fully operational in 1993, with related costs initially absorbed by the Department for Disarmament Affairs. A group of governmental experts in 1994 would be budgeted in the 1994-1995 biennium. Implementation may be subject to availability in the contingency fund; otherwise activities may be postponed per GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/SPC/46/L.9 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

Possible inter-sessional consultations for the Special Committee on Peace-keeping Operations to be serviced within permanent conference-servicing capacity on an as-available basis. stages.notes: This addendum revises the Secretary-General’s earlier PBI (A/SPC/46/L.10). Following further review, the SG now considers that all staff requirements can be met within existing resources of the Office for Special Political Affairs, with no additional requirements for salaries and common staff costs under the regular budget; any related travel costs would be covered through voluntary contributions.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.14 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Secretary-General’s statement (rule 153) on draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.14 indicates additional appropriations of $600,000 for the biennium 1992–1993 to finance the administrative costs of the three regional centres from the regular budget. First-year (1992) share extracted as $300,000. Costs comprise salaries/common staff costs for 22 local-level staff and non-staff administrative items (overtime, premises/equipment maintenance, communications, miscellaneous services, furniture/equipment). It is not anticipated that the $600,000 can be absorbed within Section 5; funding would need to come from the contingency fund or implementation may be postponed per resolutions 41/213 and 42/211. Context: two P-5 Director posts for Africa and Latin America were already authorized in the prior biennium ($457,000); a P-5 Director post for Asia and the Pacific ($114,000) was requested in the proposed 1992–1993 budget—these are noted for context and are not part of the $600,000 additional requirements under this draft resolution.
stages.budget_section: Section 5, Disarmament (subprogramme 4: Assistance to developing countries on disarmament issues: regional centres, fellowship training and advisory services)

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.14 would have the UN finance the administrative costs of the three regional disarmament centres from the regular budget. The Secretary-General estimates additional requirements under section 5 of $300,000 for 1992 (and $300,000 for 1993; biennium total $600,000). Breakdown includes salaries and common staff costs for 22 local-level staff and other non-staff administrative costs (overtime, maintenance of premises and equipment, communications, miscellaneous services, furniture and equipment). Two P-5 Director posts for Africa and Latin America were already authorized in the current biennium ($457,000), and a P-5 Director post for Asia and the Pacific was requested for 1992-1993 ($114,000); these are not part of the incremental requirements here. Costs are not anticipated to be absorbed within section 5 and may need to be met from the Contingency Fund; implementation may be postponed if the Fund cannot cover them, per GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.41 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: SG estimates additional requirements of $55,300 in 1992 to organize a five-day 1992 meeting in Addis Ababa of the UN/OAU-designated Group of Experts on the Denuclearization of Africa. If not accommodated from the contingency fund, activities could be implemented through redeployment from lower-priority disarmament outputs (e.g., some disarmament papers, bibliography issues, Disarmament Week kits, and meetings on the World Disarmament Campaign).

PBI for draft resolution A/C.1/46/L.41 (Implementation of the Declaration on the Denuclearization of Africa). One-time five-day meeting of the UN–OAU Group of Experts in Addis Ababa during 1992. UN to cover interpretation and translation, travel and DSA for 11 experts, and travel/DSA for 1 Professional and 1 General Service staff from the Department for Disarmament Affairs. OAU to provide other conference and support services at its expense. No provision exists under Section 5 for these activities; estimated additional requirements of $55,300 for 1992 to be charged to the contingency fund or, if unavailable, via redeployment from low-priority activities (e.g., disarmament papers, bibliographies, Disarmament Week kits, inter-agency meetings).

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/46/L.7 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

Programme reference: Programme 9 (International law), subprogramme 3 (Progressive development and codification of international law).

stages.notes: Sixth Committee draft resolution A/C.6/46/L.7 requests: (a) publication and wide dissemination of the Handbook on the Peaceful Settlement of Disputes between States in all six official languages; (b) a session of the Special Committee on the Charter from 3 to 21 Feb 1992 in New York; (c) a report to the GA at its 47th session. The Secretariat estimates the conference-servicing full cost for the three-week session at $296,000 (30 meetings with interpretation in six languages, pre-session, in-session, and post-session documentation). However, Section 9 already provides for substantive servicing and the Handbook’s publication, and Section 32 includes provision for temporary assistance for meetings sufficient to accommodate additional meetings consistent with past patterns. Therefore, no additional appropriation is required under sections 9 or 32 for the 1992-1993 biennium. Section 32 (Conference services) is implicated for meeting servicing but covered by existing resources.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/46/L.13 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Secretary-General requests additional net resources of $6,873,400 for 1992 to continue and intensify efforts for a comprehensive political settlement in Afghanistan (OSGAP/UNOCA-related activities). This cannot be absorbed within the proposed 1992-1993 appropriations and is to be treated outside the Contingency Fund as an extraordinary, peace and security-related requirement per GA res. 41/213. Additional appropriation to be under Section 2, plus staff assessment of $369,400 under Section 36 (fully offset by income). Two temporary posts are newly proposed for 1992: 1 P-4 interpreter/translator and 1 GS (other level) secretarial post for Peshawar. Continuation in 1992 of posts authorized in 1990-1991 (including an ASG as Alternate Personal Representative, D-1, P-5s, P-4, Field Service and local level) is included in the estimate. Provision continues for allowances and travel of 10 redeployed military officers. Offices at Islamabad, Kabul and Peshawar; continued aircraft rental with cost-recovery/miscellaneous income expected in 1992 (amount not specified). Past biennium (1990-1991) expenditures estimated at $12,821,800 vs. $13,698,600 appropriated; $876,800 reduction to be reflected in the second performance report. Note: paragraph showing $6,073,400 appears to be a typographical/OCR error; the consistent requested figure is $6,873,400.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.27 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.80 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes:
This is a Secretary-General statement under rule 153 to the Second Committee regarding draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.80. The ad hoc secretariat for the Intergovernmental Negotiating Committee on the framework convention on climate change requires 1992 resources totaling $1,296,000, of which $90,000 is expected from a negotiating-process trust fund; the remaining $1,206,000 is requested as an additional appropriation under the contingency fund, charged to Section 11. Staff (GTA) cost component is $949,000; non-staff items (consultants, overtime, expert group meeting, staff travel, communications, hospitality, general operating expenses, supplies/materials, furniture/equipment) comprise the remainder to reach the $1,296,000 total. Temporary GTA equivalents: one D-2 (head of ad hoc secretariat), one D-1 (deputy/liaison), one P-5 (senior economic officer), one P-4 (legal officer), one P-3 (documentation/conference planning liaison), one P-2 (assistant to economic/legal officers), plus GS for 60 work-months.
Conference-servicing costs for holding the fifth and resumed fifth sessions in New York total $915,500, but are to be absorbed within the Organisation’s overall conference-servicing capacity under Section 32; no additional resources are required for conference services, subject to the General Assembly granting an exception to resolution 40/243 (meeting at established headquarters; INC headquarters is Geneva).
Participation of developing countries in the two sessions is estimated at $810,000 (para. 11), to be financed from the voluntary trust fund established by GA resolution 45/212; para. 15 cites $610,000 for this item—there is an internal discrepancy in the document. Additional voluntary contributions are required and expected.
Background (1991): ad hoc secretariat staffing from WMO, UNEP, and UN Secretariat cost $2,152,000; conference servicing of four sessions cost $1,522,000 (absorbed within conference services); voluntary trust fund for participation received $1,068,000 with a $50,000 year-end balance. No redeployments or terminations identified for 1992; possible 1993 requirements to be estimated at end-1992.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.39 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: This is the Secretary-General's PBI for draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.39 concerning the International Decade for Natural Disaster Reduction. Activities are financed from extrabudgetary resources via the Trust Fund for the Decade; no additional regular budget resources are requested. Key figures for 1992-1993: (1) Trust Fund requirements for Decade secretariat staffing and operations total $1,480,400 plus 13% programme support ($192,400), for a Trust Fund total of $1,672,800 in 1992-1993. Based on an expected Trust Fund balance of $739,900 at end-1991, additional extrabudgetary resources of $932,900 are required for the biennium. (2) Non-conference-servicing costs (travel and DSA) for participants total $349,200 and are expected to be financed by UNDP. (3) Conference-servicing costs for the Special High-Level Council and the Scientific and Technical Committee total $828,700; these are to be accommodated within the Organization's overall conference-servicing capacity (Section 32) and, per the document, no additional regular budget resources are needed. Staffing details for 1992-1993: new D-2 Director post (extrabudgetary) effective Jan 1992–Dec 1993; L-6 senior scientist Jan–Jun 1992 under bilateral arrangements with the United States; P-5 senior officer Jan 1992–Dec 1993 (Trust Fund); P-4 economist for 1992 funded by Finland. The newsletter and other activities are extrabudgetary. The document notes that if voluntary contributions do not materialize, proposed activities would be deferred. Section references vary in-text (section 30 vs. 39); section 30 (Disaster relief operations) is identified as the relevant section for programming the activities. First-year-only cost breakdown is not explicitly provided; amounts are stated for the full biennium 1992-1993.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/46/L.22/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG statement on draft resolution A/46/L.22/Rev.1 for the International Decade for the Eradication of Colonialism. New money requested: $55,000 under Section 31 for information kits and upgrading photo exhibits; the statement concludes this amount cannot be absorbed and requires an additional provision. A seminar in the Caribbean region in 1992 was estimated at $523,900, but the entire amount is covered without new appropriations: $174,900 (travel/subsistence/general operating) to be absorbed under Sections 6A and 31; $349,000 (conference servicing) covered under Section 32 provision for subsequently authorized meetings. Many activities are already programmed under Section 6 (Special political questions; trusteeship and decolonization) including provisions of $618,300 under Section 6A.2 for the Special Committee. Three visits by the Secretary-General or his Special Representative during 1992-1993 are covered within Section 6. The contingency fund procedure (GA res. 41/213) applies; if funding is not available, the information activities could be postponed. The plan entails decade-long activities (multi-year nature), but the request here is for the 1992-1993 biennium without a year-by-year split; first-year amount recorded per extraction rules.

title: International Year for the World's Indigenous People : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/46/L.56 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.3/46/L.56 (International Year for the World's Indigenous People). Public information activities to begin in 1993 can be accommodated within existing resources under section 31 (Public information). A three-day technical meeting in early 1992 (six meetings with interpretation in six languages) has a theoretical full conference-servicing cost of $97,200 under section 32, but based on available temporary assistance for meetings in the 1992-1993 programme budget, these requirements are expected to be absorbed. Therefore, if the GA adopts the draft resolution, no additional appropriation would be required. Programme areas referenced: section 28 (Human rights) and section 31 (Public information).

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/46/L.67 entitled "United Nations African Institute for the Prevention of Crime and the Treatment of Offenders" : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Third Committee draft resolution requests the Secretary-General to ensure sufficient resources for UNAFRI within the 1992-1993 budget. SG assesses redeployment within section 21 (Social development, D. Crime prevention and criminal justice) or within ECA as not feasible at this stage and proposes, as an additional appropriation, a 1992 grant of $186,000 under section 23 (ECA), representing half of African member States' contribution. Use of the contingency fund would apply; if insufficient, implementation of the operative paragraph would be deferred. SG would report to the forty-seventh session on longer-term financing solutions for UNAFRI.

To implement operative paragraph 2 of draft resolution A/C.3/46/L.67, the SG indicates redeployment within Section 21 or other sections is not feasible; proposes an additional appropriation grant of $180,000 under Section 23 for 1992 to cover UNAFRI administrative costs (representing half of African member States’ contribution). Use of the contingency fund procedure would apply; if insufficient, implementation would be deferred. SG to report to the GA at its forty-seventh session on longer-term financing solutions. UNAFRI’s 1991 budget was $820,000 ($460,000 from UNDP; $360,000 from African States), and its financial situation is precarious.

title: Specific actions related to the particular needs and problems of land-locked developing countries : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.57 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Purpose: UNCTAD to undertake 10 studies, convene subregional workshops (two in ECA, one in ESCAP), and a 1993 high-level experts meeting; report to GA at its 48th session. Staff time for studies: 72 work-months (Professional) and 48 work-months (General Service) to be absorbed by adjusting the work programme; only consultancy services require additional funding. Consultancy services: $50,000 requested under section 15 for the 1992-1993 biennium (to be charged to the contingency fund per GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211). If the contingency fund cannot finance, UNCTAD proposes postponing a low-priority activity in Programme 14, Subprogramme 4 (Insurance) — parliamentary documentation/report on improving investment performance of life insurers in developing countries (six Professional work-months) — to 1994-1995 to offset. Conference servicing for the 1993 high-level meeting estimated at $196,700 if no permanent capacity were available; however, based on the conferences calendar and capacity under section 32, no additional resources are required under section 32 for 1992-1993 (absorbed). No new posts are requested.
stages.budget_section: Section 15, United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.34 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: The SG estimates an additional requirement of $62,700 in the 1992-1993 biennium under section 1 to reimburse travel costs for Zambia’s representatives to the 47th and 48th sessions of the General Assembly, following Zambia’s inclusion in the list of least developed countries. Activities related to reporting by UNCTAD (section 15) are within existing resources; no modification or additional resources are anticipated for section 15. The $62,700 is not anticipated to be absorbed within existing appropriations for section 1 and would require an additional appropriation from or through the contingency fund per resolution 41/213; if the contingency fund is insufficient, implementation may be postponed per resolution 42/211. The amount is stated for the biennium and not broken down by year.
stages.budget_section: Section 1 (travel of representatives of least developed countries to General Assembly sessions)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/46/L.55 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Initial resources identified: $100,000 for general temporary assistance and $50,000 for travel (total $150,000), to be accommodated within existing resources of Sections 2B, 6C, and 30 (UNDRO) and from extrabudgetary resources. No additional appropriation or programmatic changes to the 1992-1993 proposed programme budget at this stage. A central emergency revolving fund would be financed by voluntary contributions (outside the regular budget); donor meeting planned for Q1 1992. The Emergency Relief Coordinator post to be designated within the existing high-level posts—no new post created in the regular budget; secretariat support to be based on strengthened UNDRO and consolidation of existing offices, with staff secondments from United Nations system organizations.
stages.budget_section: Section 30 (United Nations Disaster Relief Coordinator - UNDRO)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.77 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.77 would grant a one-time US$2,000,000 to UNITAR’s General Fund as a transitional measure for training services. The SG’s PBI states this would require an additional appropriation under Section 34 in the 1992-1993 biennium, to be considered under contingency fund guidelines (per GA resolutions 41/213 and 42/211). No activities were identified for redeployment; if contingency funding is not available, no contribution would be made. ACABQ (A/46/024) noted the consultant’s view that US$2 million per annum would be needed unless adequate voluntary funding is assured and said the matter should be addressed after further system-wide determinations; no explicit ACABQ funding recommendation is provided in this PBI. Background: UNITAR’s debt to the UN was US$4.744 million as at 30 Sept. 1991 and, with land purchase advances, expected to reach about US$10.1 million by end-1991. Consultant referenced an indicative staffing (15 staff; D-2 Head) as part of a broader restructuring, but no posts are established by this draft resolution.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/46/L.25, A/46/L.31, A/46/L.32, A/46/L.41, A/46/L.42 and A/46/L.43 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 46th session

stages.notes: SG states that implementation of A/46/L.25, L.31, L.32, L.41, L.42 and L.43 requires no additional appropriation under sections 7, 31 or 32; activities will be accommodated within the proposed programme budget for 1992-1993. Key figures cited for context (within existing resources): (a) Subsection 7A (Policy-making organs) provision for the biennium: $2,575,300; (b) Subsection 7B (Centre against Apartheid) provision for the biennium: $5,609,500; (c) 1992 programmes of work of the Special Committee against Apartheid and the Intergovernmental Group: estimated full cost $567,309; based on past expenditure, estimated requirement in 1992 not to exceed $432,900 (which is the 1992 portion of a $902,500 biennial provision for regular activities); (d) Special projects of the Special Committee in 1992: draft resolution A/46/L.41 envisages a special allocation of $480,000; the 1992 portion available in the 1992-1993 provision is $455,000, with a $25,000 shortfall to be covered from the 1993 portion; (e) Grants to national liberation movements: $371,400 in 1992, fully covered by the 1992 portion of the biennial provision; (f) Summary table for 1992: total estimated full cost $1,418,700 vs resources available/programmable in 1992 of $1,259,300 (difference -$159,400), with DPI activities under section 31 funded from consingled resources and conference servicing under section 32 met from permanent resources. No new posts are proposed. The special allocation for 1992 projects is a designation within existing appropriations, not an additional appropriation.
stages.budget_section: Section 7, Policy-making organs (Special Committee against Apartheid/Centre against Apartheid)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.75 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.75 extends the fourth session of the Preparatory Committee for UNCED to five weeks (2 March–3 April 1992) and increases meetings by 75. This creates an additional conference-servicing cost of $391,000, bringing total conference-servicing for the session to $1,012,900; treated under existing procedures within the 1992-1993 programme budget. Extension also adds $34,500 for DSA for 25 secretariat representatives, to be absorbed under section 11C. Travel and per diem of $19,200 for two national liberation movements (ANC and PAC) also to be absorbed under section 11C. PLO representation via New York office (no travel cost). Therefore, no additional appropriation or programmatic changes are required.
stages.budget_section: Section 11C, United Nations Conference on Environment and Development

stages.notes: Additional conference servicing cost of $391,000 due to extending the fourth session of the UNCED Preparatory Committee to five weeks and increasing meetings by 78; total conference servicing cost becomes $1,012,900 and will be handled under existing procedures in the 1992-1993 programme budget. DSA for 23 secretariat members: $34,500 to be absorbed within Section 11C. Travel and per diem for two national liberation movements (ANC and PAC): $19,200 to be absorbed within Section 11C. PLO expected to be represented by its New York Office (no extra cost indicated).
stages.budget_section: Section 11C, United Nations Conference on Environment and Development

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/C.2/46/L.66 and A/C.2/46/L.69 and Corr. 1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Total additional cost to implement the two draft resolutions is $340,000, broken down as: consultants $250,000; general temporary assistance $75,000; operational costs $15,000. Of this, $80,000 can be absorbed within the proposed programme budget under section 11A; the remaining $260,000 would be funded from extrabudgetary resources via additional contributions to the Trust Fund for Case Studies on the Functioning of the Operational Activities for Development of the United Nations System. On this basis, no additional funding from the regular budget is required. No posts are established; GTA is short-term support without specified counts or levels.
stages.budget_section: Section 11A, Office of the Director-General for Development and International Economic Cooperation

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.121 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.121 (Operational activities of the United Nations system). The SG notes successor support cost arrangements adopted by UNDP (decision 91/32) and UNFPA (decision 91/37) are likely to impact the UN regular budget and technical cooperation scope but cannot be quantified at this stage. The proposed programme budget for 1992-1993 was prepared on the basis of current support cost reimbursement formula (GA resolution 35/217, section V). SG will monitor and advise Member States later; transitional measures exist in both arrangements. All other operative paragraphs have no programme budget implications for the regular budget for the biennium 1992-1993.

The SG states that the successor support cost arrangements adopted by UNDP (decision 91/32) and UNFPA (decision 91/37) are likely to impact the UN regular budget and technical cooperation programmes, but their impact cannot be quantified at this stage. The proposed programme budget for 1992-1993 (including programme support) was prepared on the basis of current support cost arrangements per GA resolution 35/217, section V. The SG will monitor and advise Member States later and submit recommendations as necessary. Transitional measures in the UNDP and UNFPA arrangements should facilitate introduction of the new scheme. For other operative paragraphs of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.121, the prior PBI (A/C.2/46/L.115, relating to A/C.2/46/L.66 and L.69) remains relevant. No dollar amounts or posts are requested or approved in this document.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/46/L.39 [i.e., A/C.2/46/L.39] : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG statement indicates adoption of A/C.2/46/L.39 would have no financial implications for the UN regular budget. Conference-servicing costs for the Special High-level Council and the Scientific and Technical Committee are estimated at $828,700 but are to be accommodated within the Organization’s existing conference-servicing capacity under section 32; thus no additional regular budget resources are requested. Non-conference-servicing participant travel/DSA totaling $349,200 is expected to be financed from UNDP. Decade secretariat costs to be financed from extrabudgetary Trust Fund total $1,672,800 for 1992-1993 (staff costs $810,900; operations $669,500; plus 13% programme support $192,400). With an expected Trust Fund balance of $739,900 at end-1991, additional extrabudgetary resources of $932,900 are required for 1992-1993 to maintain the secretariat and implement activities. Staffing details (extrabudgetary): proposed establishment of 1 D-2 Director (effective Jan 1992); existing temporary L-6 (senior scientist) through June 1992 funded by the United States; 1 P-5 (senior officer) funded from Trust Fund; 1 P-4 economist funded by Finland for 1992. Activities to be added under Section 30 include international cooperation support services, parliamentary documentation (extrabudgetary), recurrent bi-monthly newsletter Stop-Disasters (extrabudgetary), advisory services (extrabudgetary), and coordination with UN system and specialized agencies.

title: Report of the Committee for Development Planning : criteria for identifying the least developed countries : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.101 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Additional appropriation of $217,300 is estimated under section 1 for reimbursement of travel costs for representatives of Cambodia, Solomon Islands, Zaire, and Zambia to the forty-seventh and forty-eighth sessions of the General Assembly, due to their inclusion in the list of least developed countries. Activities under UNCTAD (section 15) are within existing resources; no additional requirements under section 15. Absorption within section 1 is not feasible; financing expected from the contingency fund, and if insufficient, implementation may be postponed. The $217,300 figure is for the 1992-1993 biennium; a first-year breakdown is not provided.
stages.budget_section: Section 1, Overall policy-making, direction and coordination

stages.notes: The SG estimates an additional appropriation of $217,277 under section 1 for travel reimbursement to GA sessions for Cambodia, Solomon Islands, Zaire, and Zambia upon their inclusion in the LDC list, covering the 47th and 48th sessions (biennium 1992-1993). This amount cannot be absorbed within the proposed appropriation and would draw on the contingency fund per GA resolution 41/213; if insufficient, implementation may be postponed per GA resolution 42/211. No additional requirements are anticipated under section 15 (UNCTAD) for the requested reports.
stages.budget_section: Section 1, Overall policy-making, direction and coordination

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/46/L.61/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Figure of $526,100 is stated as full-cost requirements for the 1992-1993 biennium; no annual (1992 vs. 1993) breakdown provided. The amount comprises: Consultants $30,000; Temporary posts $349,600; Common staff costs $134,600; Office automation equipment $11,900. Staffing envisaged: two Professional posts (P-5 and P-3) plus a secretary to support a senior official designated as focal point (the senior official serves in addition to existing duties and is not a new post). The draft resolution requests allocation from within existing resources; therefore, no additional appropriation is requested at this stage. The Secretary-General would attempt absorption via redeployments or loans; if necessary, he would seek ACABQ agreement to enter commitments in 1992 under the unforeseen and extraordinary expenses procedure. A voluntary trust fund is to be established for electoral assistance missions (extrabudgetary).

title: United Nations/International Maritime Organization Conference of Plenipotentiaries on a Draft Convention on Maritime Liens and Mortgages : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.91 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.2/46/L.91 would convene a UN/IMO Conference of Plenipotentiaries on a Draft Convention on Maritime Liens and Mortgages in Geneva in the first half of 1993 (three weeks). The SG states no modification of the programme of work or additional resources are required under Section 15 for 1992-1993. The conference is not in the draft calendar of conferences (A/46/32, annex II). Full conference-servicing cost is estimated at $1,439,000 (pre-session documentation: $361,000; meeting servicing: $643,000; in-session documentation: $176,000; post-session documentation: $179,000; Office of General Services: $80,000) and efforts would be made to absorb these costs within Section 32 (Conference services), per current practice. Participation costs for national liberation movements (ANC and PAC) are $14,700 and would be covered under Section 15; the PLO would be represented by its Geneva office. No posts are created.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/46/L.57 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: PBI for draft resolution A/46/L.57 (restructuring bodies in economic and social fields). Additional appropriation requested only under Section 14: $147,000 for travel and DSA of members of the restructured Committee on Natural Resources (one session in the 1992-1993 biennium). For the new Commission on Science and Technology for Development, travel costs of members for one session are estimated at $175,000 but are to be offset against an existing $406,000 provision under Section 18 (Centre for Science and Technology for Development) for travel of experts of subsidiary bodies; accordingly, Section 18 could be reduced by $231,000. Conference-servicing full-cost estimates per session are $496,000 for each of the two bodies, but these will be met within the existing appropriation under Section 32 (Conference services) in 1992-1993. Net of these changes across Sections 14 and 18, there is an overall reduction of $84,000. Programme budget implications for the Committee on New and Renewable Sources of Energy and on Energy for Development will be considered in the 1994-1995 budget.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/42/L.1 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 42nd session.

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decided to inform the General Assembly that, if draft resolution A/42/L.1 is adopted, no additional appropriations or programmatic changes are required for 1988-1989. Regarding a possible reconvening of the International Conference on Kampuchea (operative paragraph 5), the Secretary-General would seek to accommodate requirements within resources under sections 3 and 29; if insufficient, he would seek ACABQ concurrence to enter into the necessary commitments for the balance of the requirements. No posts or specific dollar amounts are cited.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution C contained in document A/C.5/42/L.4 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Secretary-General states implementation of draft resolution C (A/C.5/42/L.4) would have no additional financial or programmatic implications; existing resources in the proposed programme budget for 1988-1989 are sufficient to provide conference services and ensure equal treatment of official languages under existing rules and practices. Mentions potential review via revised estimates during 1988-1989 if necessary and notes possible cost reductions from technological improvements. No new posts are proposed.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/42/L.58/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

The request relates to chapter 1, programme 2, subprogramme 4 (Training in disarmament) of the medium-term plan and A/42/6 (section 2B). title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution contained in document E/1987/L.50/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 31 of the rules of procedure of the Economic and Social Council.
Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/42/L.33, A/42/L.34 and A/42/L.35 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 42nd session.

stages.notes: SG statement on PBIs for A/42/L.33, A/42/L.34 and A/42/L.35 (Question of Palestine). Conclusion: no additional appropriations required under section 1 or section 27 for 1988-1989; activities to be absorbed within proposed resources. One additional NGO symposium in Europe (1989) has a full substantive servicing cost estimate of $48,200 (or $49,900 if Vienna), but the Committee intends to implement the meetings economically to avoid additional appropriations. Conference-servicing full cost for the proposed meetings programme is estimated at $2,885,500, based on a theoretical assumption of no use of permanent capacity; temporary assistance for meetings for 1988-1989 has already been provided on a five-year average under section 29, subject to the approved calendar. DPI’s special information programme on the question of Palestine (with added emphasis in Europe and North America and additional national encounters) will be met from within section 27 resources. All listed seminars/symposiums/NGO meetings are outside the approved calendar of conferences; related operational needs (planning missions, rentals, etc.) noted in annexes.

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decided to inform the General Assembly that, should it adopt draft resolutions A/42/L.33, A/42/L.34 and A/42/L.35, no additional appropriations would be required under sections 1, 27 and 29 of the proposed programme budget for the biennium 1988-1989. Decision adopted by recorded vote (81-6-12). ACABQ and Committee on Conferences recommendations were presented orally; amounts not stated in this document.

title: Measures to uphold the authority of the 1925 Geneva Protocol and to support the conclusion of a Chemical Weapons Convention : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/42/L.67/Rev.1 and Corr.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Additional appropriation requested to engage three consultant experts (total three work-months) and support a one-week meeting in New York in 1988. stages.notes: SG statement (Sixth Committee) for draft resolution A/C.6/42/L.8. A three-week session of the Ad Hoc Committee in early 1988 (New York) is assumed. The Secretary-General indicates that, based on the five-year average approach and provisions already included in the proposed programme budget 1988-1989, no additional cost would be incurred under section 29 (Department of Conference Services); substantive servicing is already provided for under section 26 (Legal activities).

title: Measures to improve the situation and ensure the human rights and dignity of all migrant workers : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/42/L.76 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: SG statement for draft resolution A/C.3/42/L.76 indicates conference-servicing costs could be absorbed; no additional appropriation required.
