title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.66 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Estimated 1991 costs total $2,499,500: $1,522,600 for four 10‑day negotiating sessions with full six-language conference services (assumed at UN locations), and $976,900 for an ad hoc secretariat (in addition to its head: 1 D‑1, 1 P‑5, 1 P‑4, 6 GS). However, the Secretary‑General states no additional appropriations are required for the 1990‑1991 programme budget: conference servicing needs are to be absorbed within existing Section 29 temporary assistance capacity, and secretariat staff are to be seconded from UNEP, WMO and other UN bodies; the head is to be appointed from within the Secretariat. title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.93 as orally reviewed : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Overall estimated 1991 costs to implement draft resolution A/C.2/45/L.93 total $2,499,500: $1,522,600 for conference-servicing (four 10-day meetings in 1991, all six languages) and approximately $976,900 for an ad hoc secretariat (head + 1 D-1, 1 P-5, 1 P-4, 6 GS). No additional appropriations are required for the 1990-1991 programme budget: conference servicing to be met within existing temporary assistance provisions under Section 29; ad hoc secretariat staff to be made available (seconded) from UNEP, WMO and other UN bodies. First session in Washington: any extra costs for holding away from UN Headquarters to be borne by the host Government per GA resolution 40/243. Negotiating process to be funded through existing UN budgetary resources without negatively affecting programmed activities and through voluntary contributions to a dedicated trust fund for the duration of the negotiations. title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/C.3/46/L.41 and A/C.3/46/L.42 and draft decision A/C.3/46/L.47

stages.notes: No additional appropriation is required at this stage for A/C.3/46/L.41, A/C.3/46/L.42 and A/C.3/46/L.47; costs are to be absorbed within existing provisions or deferred pending treaty amendment processes. If States parties amend the Conventions (CERD and CAT) to vest funding in the UN regular budget, the Secretary-General will later submit proposals to transfer related costs. Biennial information estimates for 1992-1993 for CERD and CAT combined total $3,043,400, comprising: travel/subsistence for members (CERD $300,000; CAT $226,500), staffing requirements $675,000 (CAT staffing: 1 P-4, 1 P-2, 1 GS financed under the Convention; CERD staffing already within Section 28), four Committee sessions (two each year) $2,100,000, and a biennial meeting of States parties (1993) $39,900. Conference-servicing costs for CERD and the States parties’ biennial meeting are already included under Section 32. Biennial meeting of persons chairing human rights bodies: travel/subsistence $21,000 to be absorbed; conference servicing for a five-day meeting in 1992: $59,700. Emoluments for CERD Committee members, if decided by GA per resolutions 35/218 and 44/201 A (section VII), would be $56,000 annually (Chair $5,000; each member $3,000). For the Committee on the Rights of the Child (CRC), supporting two sessions per year and pre-sessional working groups entails additional requirements estimated at: $67,500 travel/subsistence for 10 members for a second three-week session (1992 rates); $33,500 additional travel/subsistence for five members per pre-sessional working group; $700,800 for the second three-week session in 1993 (interpretation, documentation, summary records); and $98,200 for two pre-sessional working groups in 1993 (interpretation). The 1992 working group of the whole would be accommodated by shortening the Committee’s 1992 session by one week; the meeting of States parties to determine future meeting durations would use Third Committee conference-servicing resources.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolutions A/C.3/46/L.26, A/C.3/46/L.41, A/C.3/46/L.42 and A/C.3/46/L.56 and draft decision A/C.3/46/L.47 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Document covers multiple Third Committee drafts: L.26 (World Conference on Human Rights), L.41, L.42, L.56, and draft decision L.47. Only L.26 entails an additional appropriation request. The $491,000 is a net biennial amount for 1992-1993; no breakdown by year is provided. Allocation of the $491,000: Section 28: -34,000 (decrease); Section 31: +525,000 (increase), net +491,000. A non-recurrent $1,500,000 for conference preparations had already been included under section 28 in the 1992-1993 proposed programme budget; total Secretariat and DPI requirements related to L.26 are now $1,991,000, i.e., $491,000 above that provision. Staffing (temporary/GTA equivalents for L.26): 1 P-5 (20 work-months), 2 P-4 (20 and 18 work-months), 1 P-3 (12 work-months; 6 in 1992 and 6 in 1993), and 3 GS (two at 20 work-months each and one at 18 work-months). Consultants: 36 work-months ($180,000). EDP equipment: $25,000. Public information related to World Conference: additional $410,000 (DPI products), plus $15,000 for information coverage of Preparatory Committee meetings (1992-1993), and $100,000 for Conference coverage; combined PI total shown in the statement is $525,000. Travel and hospitality: staff official travel $90,000; travel of Centre for Human Rights staff to regional meetings $20,000; hospitality $6,000; overtime $6,000; grants/prize $10,000; external printing (additional) $103,000. Conference-servicing (section 32) totals $4,049,400 for 1992-1993 (Preparatory Committees $2,100,600; regional meetings $572,400 of which $452,400 within sections 23, 24, 26 and $120,000 from section 32; pre-conference consultations $49,300; 1993 Conference $1,327,100). However, per paras. 24-25 and 55, these are to be accommodated within existing conference-servicing capacity under section 32; no new appropriation is requested under section 32. Total requirements for preparatory activities and the Conference (all components) are $6,040,400 for 1992-1993. Potential additional $400,000 could arise only if the GA decides to fund travel for chairs/rapporteurs to participate as observers (not included in current request). Venue assumption: costs estimated on Geneva; any costs above Geneva for holding the Conference in Berlin would be borne by the host country. A trust fund has been established to finance participation of least developed countries. Other drafts: L.41 and L.47 (treaty body financing and CERD Art. 8(6) amendment) and L.42 (Committee on the Rights of the Child future sessions) and L.56 (International Year for the World's Indigenous People) entail no additional appropriation at this stage; certain conference-servicing for L.56 technical meeting ($97,200) to be absorbed under section 32. First-year (1992) split of the $491,000 is not specified in the document.
stages.posts_levels: P-5, P-4, P-4, P-3, GS, GS, GS

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/47/L.58 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Request covers 1993 only. Composition of interim secretariat envisioned as 5 Professional (D-2, D-1, P-4, P-3, P-2) and 4 General Service posts; two posts (P-2 and P-3) are available for temporary redeployment; remaining seven positions (1 D-2, 1 D-1, 1 P-4, 4 GS) to be financed on general temporary assistance for six work-months each (report to ACABQ if posts not identified by 30 June 1993). Non-staff requirements in 1993 total $359,000; GTA for posts totals $379,000; combined $738,000 additional under section 11F. Conference servicing for two 10‑day 1993 sessions estimated at $990,000 at full cost, but no additional resources required under conference services (to be met within existing capacity). Participation of developing countries financed from voluntary trust fund under GA 45/212; other extrabudgetary contributions noted (e.g., $2.1 million to participation fund in 1991‑1992, $400,000 trust fund for negotiating process, $200,000 for training seminars). Requirements for 1994‑1995 to be addressed in the 1994‑1995 budget cycle.
stages.posts_levels: D-2, D-1, P-4, GS, GS, GS, GS

title: United Nations Mission in El Salvador : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/50/L.7 : 4th report of the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions.

stages.notes: Extension of MINUSAL for six months (1 Nov 1995–30 Apr 1996) with gradual reduction. Total estimated requirements $1,366,200 ($452,500 in 1995; $913,700 in 1996). $70,000 of 1995 requirements expected from MINUSAL Trust Fund; net 1995 additional appropriation under section 4: $382,500. 1996 additional appropriation under section 3: $913,700. Staff assessment amounts ($52,500 for 1994-1995; $105,000 for 1996-1997) are offset. Activities extraordinary and outside contingency fund (GA res 41/213, annex I, para. 11).
stages.posts_levels: D-2, P-5, P-4, GS, local-level, civilian police
stages.budget_section: Section 4 (1994-1995) and Section 3 (1996-1997): Peace-keeping operations and special missions

stages.notes: SG total requirement for the six-month extension of MINUSAL (1 Nov 1995–30 Apr 1996) is $1,366,200, proposed to be financed by $452,500 in 1995 and $913,700 in 1996 under the regular budget. ACABQ recommends reductions and, after adjustments, additional requirements should not exceed $367,400 for 1995 and up to $886,900 for 1996 (under Section 3). Specific ACABQ adjustments: reduce production of official reports to $5,000 total ($1,600 in 1995; $3,400 in 1996), a net reduction of $25,000 ($8,400 in 1995; $16,600 in 1996); reduce international consultant travel/subsistence by $6,700 (1995) and $10,200 (1996). ACABQ urges host Government contributions for premises and expects further staffing/cost reductions as peace accord implementation progresses; requests more detailed post justification if the Mission is extended beyond 30 April 1996. Funding for 1 May–31 Oct 1995 period was authorized under GA resolution 48/229 (unforeseen and extraordinary expenses) totaling $1,028,500; trust fund contributions to date $318,000, of which $72,090 is available to partially offset 1995 needs. Equipment transferred from ONUSAL valued at about $432,200. Additional appropriation for 1994-1995 to be considered in the second performance report; 1996 appropriation to be addressed during approval of the proposed programme budget 1996-1997. ACABQ references draft resolution A/50/L.7.
stages.posts_levels: D-2 (1), P-5 (1), P-4 (8), General Service (1), Local level (15), Civilian police (4)
stages.budget_section: Section 3 of the proposed programme budget for 1996-1997

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decision on draft resolution A/50/L.7/Rev.1 (MINUSAL): (a) Additional requirements not exceeding $367,400 under section 4 of the programme budget for the biennium 1994-1995, to be addressed in the second performance report for 1994-1995. (b) Additional amount of up to $886,900 required in 1996 for MINUSAL under section 3 of the proposed programme budget for 1996-1997, to be considered by the General Assembly in the context of approval of the proposed programme budget for 1996-1997. No posts or staff assessment mentioned. Amounts are maximums and not final appropriations pending GA approval.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/50/L.20/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: The Secretary-General states that no additional requirements arise under the proposed programme budget for 1996-1997 (section 2C.4, Disarmament). Two regular annual meetings of the Standing Advisory Committee on Security Questions in Central Africa are already programmed and financed from the regular budget. A trust fund will be established to finance other elements of the Committee's programme of work through voluntary contributions; this has no implications for the regular budget.

title: Small arms : Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/50/L.7 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Draft resolution requests the SG, within existing resources, to prepare a report assisted by a panel of 15 governmental experts. Three sessions are envisaged: June 1996 (1 week), January 1997 (2 weeks), April 1997 (2 weeks), with interpretation and documentation in six UN languages. Full-cost estimates per session: 1996: $275,800; 1997 (Jan): $384,400; 1997 (Apr): $417,900. Conference-servicing subtotals: $190,000; $272,800; $299,800. Non-conference-servicing subtotals: travel and DSA for 15 experts $77,000; $101,600; $103,000 (total $281,600) and consultant services $8,800; $10,000; $15,100 (total $33,900). The $33,900 for four work-months of consultancy is absorbed under Section 2, Political affairs (programme 7, Disarmament, subprogramme 3). Travel and DSA ($281,600) to be met from resources under Section 2.C.4, Disarmament; no additional appropriations required. Conference servicing costs to be covered under Section 26E; no additional appropriations required. Accordingly, no additional requirements arise under Sections 2C and 26E for the biennium 1996-1997. No posts are created.
stages.budget_section: Section 2.C.4, Disarmament (Section 2, Political affairs); Section 26E, Conference servicing

title: Observance of the International Year for the Eradication of Poverty and proclamation of the 1st United Nations Decade for the Eradication of Poverty : programme budget implications of draft resolution B contained in document A/C.2/50/L.39 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: The $130,600 is for a temporary P-5 post to support coordination and substantive servicing of the ad hoc open-ended working committee and Decade activities during the 1996-1997 biennium; no year-by-year breakdown is provided. Staff assessment of $29,600 (section 32) is fully offset by income and should not be counted as new money. Conference-servicing costs for two one-week sessions of the ad hoc working committee are estimated at $256,200 on a full-cost basis and will be handled within existing provision under section 26E (Conference services); actual costs will be reported in the biennium performance report. In addition, 24 work-months of GTA at P-4 under section 7A were already included in the proposed programme budget and are not part of this additional request. The Secretary-General indicates that coordination and monitoring beyond 1997 would be carried out within the Division’s core resources. Financing may draw on the contingency fund; if not available, the SG would present alternatives per resolution 42/211.
stages.budget_section: Section 7A (Department for Policy Coordination and Sustainable Development)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/50/L.14 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: SG estimates full-cost conference servicing of two Preparatory Committee sessions in 1996 at $676,400, but indicates no additional appropriations are anticipated as requirements can be absorbed within existing resources (substantive servicing under Section 6, Legal activities; conference servicing under Section 26E). Assumptions: two 3-week sessions (25 Mar–12 Apr and 12–30 Aug 1996) in New York, two meetings per day, six official languages, documentation: 10 and 30 pages pre-session for first and second sessions respectively; 60 pages in-session and 32 pages post-session for each session. Itemized per session (first/second): pre-session $14,500/$36,100; meeting servicing $230,600/$230,600; in-session $79,300/$79,300; post-session $3,000/$3,000; totals $327,400 and $349,000. No posts are created. One-time costs limited to 1996.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/50/L.61 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 50th session.

stages.notes: Full-cost estimate for enlarging the Committee on the Rights of the Child from 10 to 18 members is $518,100 per biennium, broken down as: $470,100 travel and per diem; $48,000 honorariums. However, the Secretary-General states that no additional provision is sought at this stage for 1996-1997; costs would only arise if/when the amendment is accepted by two-thirds of States Parties and enters into force, at which point the SG would revert. The SG also notes the additional requirements cannot be absorbed within section 21. Existing provision in the proposed 1996-1997 budget for the current 10-member Committee totals $681,800 ($617,800 travel/subsistence; $64,000 honorariums). Current honorarium rates: $3,000 per annum for nine members, $5,000 for the Chair. The experts are not UN staff posts; no posts are created.
Fifth Committee decided, without a vote, that should the General Assembly adopt draft resolution A/50/L.61, no additional appropriation would be required under section 21 at the present stage.

title: Establishment of the Group of Experts on Development : programme budget implications of draft resolution E/1993/L.46 : statement in accordance with rule 31 of the rules of procedure of the Economic and Social Council / submitted by the Secretary-General.

stages.notes: A provision of $327,400 is already included in the proposed programme budget for the biennium 1994-1995 under Section 8 to finance the Committee for Development Planning. The Secretary-General intends to utilize this existing provision to finance the requirements of the new Group of Experts on Development if established, with no additional resources requested at this stage. The specific activity programme and meeting schedule are unknown; if the provision proves insufficient during 1994-1995, any additional costs would be reported to the General Assembly in the final budget performance report for the biennium. No new posts are mentioned.
stages.budget_section: Section 8, Department for Policy Coordination and Sustainable Development

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/50/L.67 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decided to inform the General Assembly that, should it adopt draft resolution A/50/L.67, the Secretary-General would be authorized to enter into commitments of $2,000,000 gross ($1,800,000 net) for the period 8 Feb 1996 to 31 May 1996, contingent on extension beyond 7 Feb 1996. The Committee requested the Secretary-General to submit, by 15 May 1996, proposals on possible means of absorption within the 1996-1997 programme budget (including Part II), and decided to revert to appropriations at its May 1996 session. GA resolution 50/214 had requested economies totaling $103,991,200.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/51/L.15 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: SG estimates full-cost requirements of $171,800 for servicing a subregional conference at Brazzaville in January 1997 (conference-servicing $26,500; non-conference-servicing $125,500; programme support costs 13% $19,800). However, the SG indicates these requirements are expected to be financed from the Trust Fund for the UN Standing Advisory Committee on Security Questions in Central Africa (voluntary contributions), with no additional requirements under the regular budget (subsection 2C.4) for 1996-1997. Trust Fund contributions to date (since March 1996) total $602,932; $209,000 was used for a Peace Operations Training Seminar in September 1996, with further missions and 1997 meetings planned. Regular-budget servicing of Standing Advisory Committee meetings was reduced from two per year to one due to the UN financial crisis. Assistance to States members and the required report to the GA are to be carried out to the extent possible within existing section 2 resources.
stages.budget_section: Section 2, Political affairs (subsection 2C.4, Disarmament; Programme 7, Subprogramme 4)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/51/L.39 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: No specific additional resource requirements or post creations are quantified. The draft resolution requests that additional human and financial resources be made available "from within the overall regular budget" to enhance OHCHR/Centre for Human Rights capacity, but the Secretary-General’s statement emphasizes that the Fifth Committee and ACABQ determine resource levels. The document cites overall 1996-1997 budget figures: provisional total $2,712,265,200, projected savings $103,991,200, and total level of expenditures $2,608,274,000. The Secretary-General submitted report A/C.5/50/57/Add.1 identifying reductions to reach $2.608 billion, under consideration by the Fifth Committee, which will decide on allocation among sections.
stages.budget_section: United Nations human rights programme (High Commissioner for Human Rights and Centre for Human Rights)

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/51/L.20 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Full-cost estimate for conference-servicing the Special Committee session (27 Jan–7 Feb 1997). The meeting would be additional to the approved calendar of conferences for 1997; accommodating it is under review and subject to a Fifth Committee recommendation. Requirements to be considered with the first performance report for 1996-1997 in Dec 1996.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/51/L.10 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: SG estimates conference-servicing requirements at full cost for Preparatory Committee sessions: 1997 total $1,179,800 (Feb, Aug, Dec sessions) and 1998 $534,600 (Mar–Apr session). The 1997 sessions are additional to the approved 1996-1997 calendar; the 1998 session would be incorporated into the draft 1998-1999 calendar. Accommodation of the additional 1997 meetings in the calendar is subject to Fifth Committee recommendation and consideration with the first performance report for 1996-1997. Dates noted were still under consideration.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/51/L.16 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: One-time conference-servicing costs to convene a second session of the Working Group of the Whole (24 March–4 April 1997), two meetings per day with interpretation in six official languages; documentation: 10 pages pre-session, 30 pages in-session, 30 pages post-session in all six languages. Costs are in addition to the approved calendar of conferences; accommodation of the additional meeting in the 1997 calendar is under review and subject to the Fifth Committee’s recommendation and consideration in the first performance report for 1996-1997. Cost breakdown (USD): pre-session 12,600; meeting servicing 201,700; in-session documentation 40,700; post-session documentation 33,900; total 288,900.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.4/51/L.9 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: The Secretary-General concludes that adoption of draft resolution A/C.4/51/L.9 does not give rise to programme budget implications for the 1996-1997 biennium. Operative paragraph 4 of the draft resolution specifies that any resulting additional costs for 1996-1997 or 1998-1999 should be accommodated within existing appropriation levels (absorption). An estimated $176,100 is noted for translation/reproduction/distribution of Lessons Learned Unit studies, but these costs are to be funded from voluntary contributions, not the regular budget. Current extrabudgetary staffing of the Lessons Learned Unit: 4 staff (2 P-4, 1 P-3, 1 GS) funded from a trust fund; no new regular-budget posts are proposed or approved. There are 14 gratis personnel on loan working on troop-reimbursement activities; future financing to be considered in the broader review of gratis personnel. Earlier proposal to convert 19 posts from the peacekeeping support account to the regular budget was not approved by the GA. Contextual appropriation level for 1996-1997: $2,608,274,000 (with provisional total $2,712,265,200 and projected savings $103,991,200), but these figures are context and not new PBI amounts.
stages.budget_section: Programme 1, Good offices, preventive diplomacy, peacemaking, peacekeeping (subprogramme 2: Peacekeeping operations)

title: United Nations political presence in El Salvador : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/51/L.58 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Support unit in El Salvador for six months (1 Jan–30 Jun 1997) to assist a high-level envoy; ONUV closed on 31 Dec 1996. Requirements total $408,700 for 1997 (net, excluding staff assessment). Additional staff assessment of $68,200 under section 32 is fully offset by income section 1. Staffing for the support unit comprises 4 international posts (1 P-4 Legal Officer, 1 P-3 Political Affairs Officer, 1 P-2 Political Affairs Officer, 1 GS), plus 7 Local level staff and 1 civilian police (mission allowance and travel included in costs). Administrative support provided by UNDP. Activities are extraordinary (outside contingency fund procedures per GA resolution 41/213, annex I, para. 11). No capacity for absorption under section 3.
stages.budget_section: Section 3, Peacekeeping operations and special missions

stages.notes: Fifth Committee decided to inform the GA that, if draft resolution A/51/L.58 is adopted, additional requirements of $408,700 would arise for 1997 under Section 3. Staff assessment of $68,200 under Section 32 would be fully offset by the same amount under Income Section 1 (Income from staff assessment). Requirements to be considered by the GA in the context of revised appropriations for the 1996-1997 biennium. ACABQ report was presented orally; no separate ACABQ recommendation amount is stated in this document.
stages.budget_section: Section 3, Peacekeeping operations and special missions

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/52/L.6 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: The Secretary-General indicates no additional requirements under the regular budget for implementing the draft resolution A/C.1/52/L.6; activities will be carried out within existing resources of Section 2B (transfer from Programme 26, Disarmament). The Standing Advisory Committee’s programme activities for 1997–1998 are expected to be funded from voluntary contributions to the Trust Fund. Specific trust-fund costs cited: (1) Subregional conference 'Democratic institutions and peace in Central Africa' previously provided for at $171,800 (inclusive of 13% programme support cost). (2) A joint meeting of ministers of defense and interior in Libreville and publication of a reference document estimated at $105,100 (includes conference servicing, travel and DSA for participants and staff, printing, GOE, and 13% PSC of $12,100). Trust Fund balance (excluding the conference provision) was $106,000 and would be depleted by the $105,100 if no further contributions are received. The proposed programme budget for 1998–1999 already provides for substantive servicing of two annual ministerial meetings of the Standing Advisory Committee estimated at $500,000; substantive servicing of four plenary sessions is included per para. 2.128(a)(xi) of the proposed programme budget. No additional requirements would arise under Section 2 (proposed transfer to new Section 2B).

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.6/52/L.15 and Corr.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Secretary-General provided two cost options for implementing draft resolution A/C.6/52/L.15 and Corr.1 concerning International Law Commission sessions in 1998-1999. Option A (all sessions in Geneva): additional requirements under Section 6 are $157,900 in 1998 and $117,700 in 1999 (biennium total cited variously as approx. $275,500–$275,600; Annex I shows 1998: $157,900; 1999: $117,700). Option B (1998 split session: 8 weeks Geneva, 3 weeks New York; 1999 all Geneva): additional requirements under Section 6 are $127,500 in 1998 and $117,700 in 1999 (biennium total $245,200; Annex II confirms amounts). The split-session option would require an exception to GA resolution 40/243 (which states ILC sessions shall be held at UNOG). Conference-servicing costs (Section 27E) are already provided for in the proposed programme budget; no additional resources are anticipated there. Base provision in the proposed programme budget already included two 10-week sessions at Geneva, with $1,851,100 for travel/per diem of members and staff and honoraria; the above figures are additional to that base provision.

Draft resolution A/C.6/52/L.15 and Corr.1 concerns the International Law Commission (ILC) session arrangements and duration for 1998–1999. The Secretary-General provides two costing options: (1) All sessions at Geneva: 1998 (11 weeks) net additional under section 6 = $157,900; 1999 (12 weeks) = $117,700; biennium total = $275,600 (Annex I). (2) Split session in 1998 (8 weeks Geneva, 3 weeks New York) with 1999 at Geneva: 1998 net additional under section 6 = $127,500; 1999 = $117,700; biennium total = $245,200 (Annex II). Conference-servicing costs are provided for under section 27E of the proposed programme budget; additional conference service resources are not anticipated—incremental resources are for substantive servicing under section 6 (travel/per diem of members and staff, honoraria, Chairman’s travel). Holding part of a session away from Geneva requires an exception to GA resolution 40/243. Baseline provision in the proposed programme budget covered two 10-week sessions at Geneva with $1,851,100 for travel and honoraria. Note: There are minor rounding discrepancies in the narrative (e.g., para. 7 cites $157,815/$117,713, total $275,528; para. 10 cites $275,500). Annex figures are used here as authoritative.

title: United Nations Verification Mission in Guatemala : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/52/L.19 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: ACABQ reviewed the SG’s statement A/C.5/52/21 on draft resolution A/52/L.19 (extension of MINUGUA). It notes total estimated costs for 21 months (1 Apr 1998–31 Dec 1999) of $66,654,800 gross or $61,815,100 net, and recommends no reduction in the SG’s estimates; an additional appropriation of $61,815,100 (net) under Section 3 would be required for 1 Apr 1998–31 Dec 1999. Staff assessment of $4,839,700 under Section 32 is to be fully offset by income (Income section 1). Requirements for 1 Jan–31 Mar 1998 ($8,372,300) are already in the proposed programme budget. ACABQ expects economies based on its observations (paras. 10, 11, 14, 17, 19), partially offset by adding provision for civilian police death and disability and possible higher rotation travel costs. Key staffing recommendations: concur to establish two new subregional offices (Fray Bartolomé de las Casas and Chimaltenango) with costs totalling $784,800 ($404,600 and $380,200) and to staff each with 1 P-3 political officer and 4 local support staff; concur to establish two mobile verification teams (local interpreters/drivers; numbers not specified); recommend converting only 8 United Nations Volunteer posts to United Nations staff (vs. SG’s proposal for 16; grades not specified); recommend maintaining heads of five verification areas at P-5 (reject upgrade to D-1); concur to grade public information at P-4 (instead of P-5) and recruit 8 national officers for public information (one per regional office). Concur to increase civilian police observers by 13 (from 38 to 51) and military liaison officers by 3 (from 17 to 20). Financial observations: reduce mission subsistence allowance provision by $143,700 per GA resolution 51/218 E; helicopter operations revised contract reduces cost by $609,000 (new fixed monthly rate $82,000 for 60 hours + $500 third-party liability), revised total air operations cost $2,720,800; vehicle replacement: non-recurrent $3,875,500 to replace 171 of 223 vehicles; communications equipment non-recurrent $158,700; other equipment non-recurrent $618,700. ACABQ questions sharp increase in vehicle maintenance from $128,400 to $826,800 and communications maintenance $306,500 given large replacements. Civilian police travel provision $159,000 (10 rotations in 1998, 30 in 1999) may be underestimated. ACABQ requests adequate provision for civilian police death/disability (none included). As of 31 Oct 1997 there were 25 vacancies among 434 approved posts; annex II shows totals of 444 (1997–Mar 1998) rising to 470 (Apr 1998–Dec 1999) excluding civilian police and military liaison. ACABQ requests a performance report by end-1998.
stages.posts_levels: P-3 political officer (2), Local support staff (8), National Officer (8), Civilian police observers (13), Military liaison officers (3)
stages.budget_section: Section 3, Peacekeeping operations and special missions

stages.notes: Addendum adjusts the cost to reflect a shortened MINUGUA mandate (1 Apr–31 Dec 1998). Net amount is $28,200,000 (gross $29,962,800). Additional appropriation under section 3 of the 1998-1999 proposed programme budget; staff assessment $1,752,800 under section 32 to be offset by income under section 1. This replaces the earlier estimate of $61,815,100 net for 21 months in A/C.5/52/21.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/52/L.43 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.

stages.notes: Special session of the GA on ICPD review to be held 30 June–2 July 1999. Four meetings per day, full services with verbatim records for plenary only; approx. 176 pages of documentation in six languages. Conference servicing ($396,200) and related support services ($109,000) are covered within existing provision under sections 27D (Support services) and 27E (Conference services); thus no additional appropriations for those. Additional new requirement is $120,000 for round-trip travel of one representative from each least developed country, under section 7A; this cannot be absorbed within section 7A resources and may need contingency fund coverage or reprogramming if the fund is insufficient.

Special session (30 June–2 July 1999) to be serviced with four meetings per day, full services, verbatim records for plenary only; 176 pages of documentation in six languages. Full-cost requirements estimated at $396,200 (conference servicing) and $109,000 (related support services), but no additional appropriations required for 1998-1999 as needs can be accommodated within Sections 27D/27E. Estimated $120,000 for travel of one representative from each LDC to be funded from extrabudgetary resources (not from the regular budget). No additional requirements under Section 7A (Economic and social affairs).

The document does not provide a first-year (1998) breakdown; amounts cited are biennium totals.

title: Use of mercenaries as a means of impeding the exercise of the right of peoples to self-determination :#programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/54/L.27 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Biennium 2000-2001 SG estimate under section 22 totals $184,600, comprised of: GTA 3 work months at P-3 ($31,000) and 3 work months at GS-Other ($23,100); one consultant for 2 work months ($25,000); travel and DSA for 20 academic experts ($105,500). This amount would be a charge against the contingency fund; no activities identified for absorption within section 22. Conference-servicing estimates for Geneva (section 2) total $435,000 at full cost (meeting services $65,600; pre-session documentation 20 docs/300 pages $220,600; in-session 10 docs/100 pages $74,400; post-session 10 docs/100 pages $74,400), but no additional appropriation is required under section 2 as requirements are expected to be met within existing capacity. Activities include preparation of a booklet on mercenaries’ adverse effects and two 5-day expert meetings in Geneva (10 academic experts per meeting). Amount shown is presented in the document as a biennial requirement; no first-year split is provided.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/54/L.73 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Secretary-General identifies additional requirements totaling $646,400 for the 2000-2001 biennium: $541,400 for 48 work months of GTA (equivalent to 1 P-5, 1 P-4, 2 P-3) to strengthen the Office of the Special Coordinator (para. 16); $50,000 for staff travel to service two sessions of the intergovernmental preparatory committee in New York (para. 2); $20,000 for staff travel and $5,000 miscellaneous for two regional preparatory events with ECA and ESCAP (para. 4); and $30,000 for additional public information to enhance awareness (para. 13). Of the total, $616,400 would be under section 11.A (Trade and development) and $30,000 under section 26 (Public information), to be charged to the contingency fund and require additional appropriation. No provision for absorption was identified. Change of venue of the Conference from Geneva to Brussels: any additional costs to be borne by the host (EU) per GA res. 40/243 (para. 4). Participation costs for two representatives from each LDC in the preparatory committee sessions and the Conference are to be met from extrabudgetary resources; if insufficient, SG would first use remaining unspent balances from 1996-1997 (about $576,700 available) as an exceptional measure (para. 14); this implies an exception to GA res. 1798 (XVII). Meetings in New York are an exception to GA res. 40/243. Amounts are presented for the biennium; the document does not provide a year-by-year breakdown. Section 26 is also affected for public information ($30,000).

ACABQ recommends additional provisions of $616,400 under section 11A (covering $541,400 in general temporary assistance totaling 48 work-months: 1 P-5, 1 P-4, 2 P-3; $70,000 staff travel; $5,000 miscellaneous) and $30,000 under section 26 (public information), to be charged to the contingency fund. The cost of participation of two representatives from each least developed country in two preparatory committee sessions and the Conference is estimated at $1.7 million and is to be met from extrabudgetary resources; if insufficient, the SG referenced an unspent balance of $576,700 from GA resolution 53/3 (1996-1997), but ACABQ notes that resolution 53/3 covers expert meetings and not the activities outlined, and suggests GA consider using savings from the 1998-1999 programme budget or making provision in 2000-2001. Depending on GA action on financing participation costs, there may be an additional charge against the contingency fund. Any additional costs from a venue change to Brussels would be borne by the host (European Union). Payment of travel to government representatives would be an exception to GA resolution 1798 (XVII).

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/54/L.62 :#the equality of all five successor States to the former Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia : statement in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly / submitted by the Secretary-General

stages.notes: No additional resource requirements or posts are proposed. If A/54/L.62 is adopted, the 2000 regular budget scale of assessments would exclude Yugoslavia's 0.026% rate and, after adding the three Member States admitted in 1999, total 99.977% rather than 100%. A new 100% scale for 2001-2003 would be considered next year (including Kiribati, Nauru, Tonga and excluding Yugoslavia). Outstanding assessed contributions attributed to Yugoslavia total $15,510,279 as at 13 December 1999. There is an advance of $60,000 attributed to Yugoslavia in the Working Capital Fund and a credit of $1,846 in the UNTAG special account. The General Assembly would need to decide on the disposition of these assets and liabilities.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/54/L.73 :#implementation of the Programme of Action for the Least Developed Countries for the 1990s : statement in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly / submitted by the Secretary-General

stages.notes: Secretary-General identifies total additional regular budget requirements of $646,400 for the biennium 2000-2001, to be charged to the contingency fund. Breakdown: (a) Staff travel for two sessions of the intergovernmental preparatory committee in New York: $50,000; (b) Two regional-level preparatory events: staff travel $20,000 and miscellaneous expenses $5,000; (c) Public information to enhance awareness (in addition to $163,600 already proposed for conference coverage under section 26): $30,000; (d) General temporary assistance: 48 work months equivalent to one P-5, one P-4 and two P-3: $541,400. Section allocations: $616,400 under section 11A (Trade and development) and $30,000 under section 26 (Public information). No absorption was identified under sections 11A or 26; the amounts would require an additional appropriation against the contingency fund. Extrabudgetary items (not regular budget): participation costs for two government representatives from each LDC estimated at $1,164,500 for the preparatory committee meetings and $500,500 for the Conference; any shortfall may use the remaining $576,700 from the unspent balance authorized by GA resolution 53/3; travel payments imply an exception to GA resolution 1798 (XVII). Additional costs of relocating the Conference from Geneva to Brussels are to be borne by the host (European Union), per GA resolution 40/243. The meetings in New York constitute an exception to the headquarters meeting principle in GA 40/243 and are subject to conference servicing availability.

title: Preparations for the substantive preparatory process and the high-level international event :#programme budget implications of draft resolution A/54/L.82 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG estimates additional requirements of $154,500 in 2000 for travel and DSA related to Bureau consultations and two sets of hearings; conference servicing for two substantive sessions in 2001 to be met within existing resources on an as-available basis (no new resources). Breakdown of $154,500: Washington consultations (3 trips of 15-member Bureau + 2 Secretariat staff): $18,800; Geneva consultations (1 trip of 15-member Bureau + 1 Secretariat staff): $29,100; five regional commissions consultations (1 trip by 3 Bureau members + 1 Secretariat staff to each of 5 commissions): $71,500; two sets of hearings in New York (invite five individuals, one from each region, per hearing): $35,100. Under section 9 of the 2000-2001 proposed programme budget, $60,000 had already been approved for consultancy services for the Ad Hoc Open-ended Working Group (context, not new money here). SG proposes authorization to incur up to $154,500 and to report actual requirements in the first performance report; if additional resources are required, recourse to the contingency fund. Hearings during the GA regular session (Sep-Dec 2000) require explicit Assembly authorization per GA resolution 40/243 and scheduling as facilities/services become available. Payment of travel and subsistence to Bureau members would imply a waiver of GA resolution 1798 (XVII) provisions regarding such payments to members of UN organs/subsidiary organs.

The statement recalls prior oral PBI (annex) under draft A/54/L.81/Rev.1 indicating additional security $450,000 and protocol $193,000; paragraph 9 notes the OCSS/DPI amounts are in addition to these security/protocol costs, although the action requested in this document quotes only the $1,111,900–$1,401,600 range. Meetings to be held in the ECOSOC chamber; plenary servicing covered under normal Assembly servicing (no additional conference servicing for plenary).

title: Critical situation of the International Research and Training Institute for the Advancement of Women : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/56/L.20/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Breakdown of the $96,000 GTA for the working group: six work-months of P-4 Professional staff ($67,000) and six work-months of General Service staff ($29,000). For INSTRAW core operations in 2002, an additional $650,000 from the regular budget is estimated, given projected expenditures of ~$750,000 and expected voluntary contributions of ~$100,000. Earlier summary language referenced $600,000 annually for core operations; the detailed 2002 estimate is $650,000. Staff assessment of $22,300 (section 32) is fully offset by income section 1 and is not new money. The modality of providing assistance to INSTRAW was to be decided by the Fifth Committee in the context of the 2002-2003 programme budget. Prior GA actions noted: resolution 55/219 and decision 55/457 (one-time advance up to $800,000 for 2001), with any unused balance potentially to be surrendered unless the GA decided otherwise; ECOSOC resolution 2001/40 recommended transferring any remaining balance of the $800,000 as a reserve for 2002.

title: United Nations Institute for Training and Research : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/56/L.30 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Secretary-General states a biennial appropriation of $292,600 would be required under the 2002-2003 programme budget (A/56/6 (Sect. 30)), implying $146,300 in the first year (2002). Costs relate to reclassifying UNITAR rent, maintenance, and other administrative support in New York and Geneva. Model analogous to UNRISD (regular budget assumes rent/maintenance; UNITAR pays 8% overhead on General Fund expenditures, estimated at $77,720 for 2001, partially offsetting administrative services). Adoption would require overriding GA resolution 47/227 (para. 3) and effectively making an exception to GA resolution 41/213/recommendation 36; amendment to UNITAR Statute article VIII, para. 13, is proposed. Modality of reclassification to be addressed by the Fifth Committee. Outstanding UNITAR arrears ($186,540) are noted but not part of new appropriations.

title: Programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/56/L.6 : report of the 5th Committee : General Assembly, 56th session

stages.notes: Fifth Committee recommended that adoption of A/C.2/56/L.6 would not require any additional appropriation for the 2002-2003 biennium; arrangements for the President of ECOSOC to be modeled on those for the President of the Security Council.

title: Comprehensive implementation of the outcome of and follow-up to the World Conference against Racism, Racial Discrimination, Xenophobia and Related Intolerance : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/56/L.84/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Secretary-General states additional requirements for biennium 2002-2003 totaling $1,178,700, comprising $1,074,800 under section 22 (Human rights) plus $103,900 staff assessment (offset by income, not new money). Establishes an Anti-Discrimination Unit with 3 Professional (1 P-5 Coordinator; 2 P-3 Research Officers) and 1 General Service Programme Assistant; vacancy rates applied for estimation (50% Professional, 65% GS). Cost plan (section 22) includes: posts $442,800; other staff costs $18,200; experts: five eminent independent experts’ travel and DSA for three Geneva meetings $63,900; five regional meetings travel and DSA $455,600 (total experts-related $519,500); staff travel (regional meetings and New York) $70,500; general operating expenses (maintenance of office and EDP equipment) $22,600; supplies and materials $1,200. Activities funded include three meetings of five independent eminent experts (two in 2002, one in 2003), five regional expert meetings (two in 2002, three in 2003), annual reporting to GA and Commission, database work, and liaison/coordination tasks. Small grants scheme for national projects to be funded from extrabudgetary resources (not part of regular budget). SG indicates requirements cannot be absorbed within section 22 and would be a charge against the contingency fund; if not met, reprogramming/modification of section 22 activities would be required.

Secretary-General states additional requirements total $1,178,700 for the biennium 2002-2003, comprising $1,074,800 under section 22 (Human rights) plus $103,900 for staff assessment (offset by income). Amount shown here ($1,074,800) is the section 22 requirement; the document does not provide a first-year (2002) breakdown. Charge is against the contingency fund; not anticipated to be absorbed within existing section 22 resources. New Anti-Discrimination Unit includes 1 P-5 Coordinator, 2 P-3 Research Officers, and 1 General Service post; costs estimated with vacancy rates of 50% (Professional) and 65% (GS) for new positions in 2002-2003. Other planned costs include meetings of five eminent experts, five regional meetings, staff travel, and general operating expenses.

Fifth Committee draft decision endorses ACABQ recommendations (A/56/869) regarding PBI for A/C.3/56/L.84/Rev.1 (World Conference against Racism follow-up). Recommends authorization of commitment authority up to $1,074,800 under Section 22 for the 2002-2003 biennium. Staff assessment of $103,900 (Section 32) is fully offset by income. Secretary-General to report at the 57th session on actual requirements; final action to be taken then by the Assembly.

title: Programme budget for the biennium 2002-2003 : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.5/56/L.47 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG statement on draft resolution A/C.5/56/L.47 provides no dollar figure for additional resource requirements. The SG states that if appropriations remain at $2,625,178,700, the measures reducing services cannot be discontinued, and it is not possible to propose implementation without affecting services. No posts are mentioned and no specific new funding request is made in this document.

stages.notes: Additional conference-servicing costs are estimated at $137,900 under Section 2 (GAACS) and $23,300 under Section 27F (Administration, Vienna), totaling $161,200 for the 2002-2003 biennium. Substantive servicing will be carried out within existing resources under Section 14 (Crime prevention and criminal justice). title: United Nations regional centres for peace and disarmament : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/57/L.11 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Support to UN regional centres for peace and disarmament to be provided within existing resources under Section 4; activities fall under subprogramme 5 (Regional disarmament). title: United Nations Regional Centre for Peace, Disarmament and Development in Latin America and the Caribbean : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.1/57/L.16 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

title: Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.7 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: SG statement under rule 153 for draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.7 proposes a phased establishment of the Permanent Forum Secretariat. 2003 net additional appropriation requested: $382,000 (Sections 9 and 27D combined; staff assessment of $42,500 is fully offset). Breakdown for phased option (2003): Section 9 costs include posts ($198,800 at vacancy rates), other staff costs $15,000, consultants $20,000, travel $20,000, contractual services $10,000, general operating expenditures $10,500, supplies $1,200; Section 27D includes rental of premises $37,900 and alterations/improvements $58,300. An additional $30,800 (Section 9) is requested for DSA for 16 Forum members for an exceptional three‑day pre‑sessional meeting (7–9 May 2003). Conference-servicing for that meeting is estimated at $98,000 under Section 2, to be accommodated within existing resources; no additional resources requested at this stage for Section 2. The Secretariat plans a total of six posts for the Forum secretariat (one D‑1, one P‑4, one P‑3, one P‑2, and two GS); the remaining three posts (P‑3, P‑2, GS) are to be proposed in the 2004‑2005 budget. Implementation depends on contingency fund availability; Third Committee is invited to prioritize: first the secretariat ($382,000), second the pre‑sessional meeting ($30,800).

SG proposes phased establishment of the Permanent Forum secretariat in DESA: 2003 creation of 3 posts (D-1, P-4, GS) with resource needs of $382,000, plus $30,800 for DSA for an exceptional three-day pre-sessional meeting (7–9 May 2003), totaling up to $412,800 in 2003. Conference-servicing costs for the meeting estimated at $98,000 under Section 2 to be accommodated within existing resources (no additional appropriation requested at present). Alternative full establishment in 2003 of six posts would total $624,400 (including Section 27D premises costs; staff assessment is offset). Additional premises costs under Section 27D are included in the $382,000 figure (rental and alterations/improvements). Staff assessment (Section 32) of $43,000 is fully offset by income and not new money. If contingency fund resources are insufficient, one or both activities may not be implemented; Third Committee did not indicate priorities. OCHR cannot redeploy existing indigenous-related resources to cover this request. Remaining three posts (P-3, P-2, GS) proposed for establishment as of 1 January 2004 in the 2004–2005 budget.

ACABQ reviewed SG statement A/C.5/57/26 on draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.7. It recommends approval, for 2003, of the first-stage establishment of three posts for the Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues secretariat (D-1, P-4, GS) and associated costs. Total 2003 implications are stated as up to $412,800, comprising $382,000 for establishing the secretariat and $30,800 for a three-day pre-sessional meeting (DSA for Forum members, 7–9 May 2003). ACABQ advises that the second-stage posts (P-3, P-2, GS) be considered in the 2004-2005 proposed programme budget. It calls for clarification of roles between DESA and OHCHR to avoid duplication and optimize resources. The amount would represent a potential charge against the contingency fund. Costs pertain to the 2002-2003 biennium but are incurred in 2003.
stages.budget_section: Department of Economic and Social Affairs

stages.notes: Fifth Committee endorsed ACABQ recommendations and informed the GA that, should it adopt draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.7, there would be implications in the biennium 2002-2003 of up to $316,600 under section 9 (Economic and social affairs) and $96,000 under section 27 (Management and central support services). A related $43,000 under section 32 (Staff assessment) is fully offset by $43,000 under income section 1 (Income from staff assessment). Amounts are stated as 'up to' and are contingent on GA adoption of the draft resolution.

title: Optional Protocol to the Convention against Torture and Other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.30 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Secretary-General states no programme budget implications for biennium 2002-2003. Secretariat support: 2004—1 P-4 and 1 GS; 2005—1 P-4, 1 P-3, and 1 GS. Costs span Section 22 (Human rights), Section 2 (DGACM conference servicing), and Section 27 (Administration/common services). Figures are contingent on the Protocol’s entry into force (after the 20th ratification/accession).

title: Future operations of the International Research and Training Institute for the Advancement of Women : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.16/Rev.1 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Total estimated annual need: $1,809,500, comprising: $500,000 for four existing Professional posts (one D-2, one P-4, one P-3, one P-2/1); $176,000 for a new Deputy Director post; $285,000 for four existing General Service posts; $208,500 for minimal administrative/operating expenses; $400,000 for minimal substantive activities (consultancy, travel, GTA); and $240,000 for a New York liaison unit (one Professional and one General Service post: $202,800; office space/equipment/supplies/travel: $37,200). The $500,000 would be an additional appropriation under the contingency fund (resolutions 41/213 and 42/211) for 2003; the remaining $1,309,500 is expected from voluntary contributions. No existing resources are available in the 2002-2003 programme budget to finance INSTRAW operations in 2003; the Fifth Committee will decide how to meet the requirements.

stages.notes: Draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.16/Rev.1 would require $500,000 from the 2002-2003 regular budget (charged to the contingency fund) to finance INSTRAW operations in 2003; an additional $1,309,500 is expected from voluntary contributions. Total estimated annual needs are $1,809,500, comprising: $500,000 for four existing Professional posts (1 D-2, 1 P-4, 1 P-3, 1 P-2/1); $176,000 for a new Deputy Director; $285,000 for four existing General Service posts; $208,500 for minimal administrative/operating costs; $400,000 for minimal substantive activities; and $240,000 for a New York liaison unit (one Professional and one GS plus related operating costs). No resources can be absorbed within the approved 2002-2003 programme budget; the $500,000 would be a potential charge against the contingency fund.

stages.notes: SG’s statement projected total annual resource needs of $1,809,500, of which $500,000 would come from the regular budget to finance core activities/4 existing Professional posts (1 D-2, 1 P-4, 1 P-3, 1 P-2/1); the remaining $1,309,500 to be covered by voluntary contributions (including a new Deputy Director post, 4 GS posts, and operating costs). ACABQ indicates that, should the draft be adopted, an additional $500,000 is required for the 2002-2003 biennium under section 9, as a potential charge against the contingency fund. 2002 operations had been ensured via $650,000 retained from the 2000-2001 budget; thus the regular-budget need effectively covers 2003. ACABQ also expresses trust that $1,309,500 in voluntary contributions would be realized.

Fifth Committee informs the GA that, should it adopt draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.16/Rev.1 (INSTRAW), a provision of US$ 500,000 would be required under Section 9 for the 2002-2003 biennium, charged to the contingency fund. The Committee recommends the GA: (a) approve a charge of $250,000 against the contingency fund to finance core activities, defined as salaries and common staff costs of four Professional posts currently in the staffing table; (b) set aside an additional $250,000 in the contingency fund for core activities in 2003 (bringing the total potential provision to $500,000), pending firm pledges/payments of voluntary contributions necessary for INSTRAW’s viable operation; and (c) consider the additional $250,000 charge after the Secretary-General reports on the Institute’s financial situation during the resumed fifty-seventh session. No new posts are created; funding relates to existing four Professional posts.

title: Khmer Rouge trials : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.70 : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Cost covers a two-week expert mission to Cambodia in January 2003 to support report preparation on Extraordinary Chambers; substantive support by Office of Legal Affairs and Department of Political Affairs. Costs relate to sending a team of experts to Cambodia for two weeks in January 2003 to prepare the Secretary-General’s report. Covers travel and DSA only; substantive support by OLA and DPA. ACABQ recommends that the $44,800 requirement for a two-week expert mission to Cambodia (travel and DSA) be absorbed within existing resources under section 8. Any need for additional appropriation could be addressed in the second performance report. Substantive support from OLA and DPA. Subject to adoption of draft resolution A/C.3/57/L.70.

Fifth Committee endorsed ACABQ (A/57/7/Add.22) and decided that, if GA adopts A/C.3/57/L.70 (Khmer Rouge trials), requirements would be met from within existing resources under Section 8, Legal affairs, of the 2002-2003 programme budget. No new posts or specific dollar amounts are authorized; absorption implies $0 in additional appropriations.

title: Ensuring effective secretariat support for sustained follow-up to the outcome of the International Conference on Financing and Development : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.2/57/L.62 : 25th report of the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions

stages.notes: ACABQ reviewed SG statement A/C.5/57/32 on draft resolution A/C.2/57/L.62. It recommends proceeding to fill the D-2 head of the new Financing for Development Office. ACABQ indicates additional expenditures of $203,200 would arise under the 2002-2003 programme budget (starting at the beginning of 2003), representing a potential charge against the contingency fund. The Office is within DESA and envisaged to consolidate finance aspects of development through redeployment for the 2002-2003 biennium. Proposed office staffing totals 19 posts (14 Professional, 5 GS): 13 redeployed regular budget posts (10 Professional: 2 D-1, 2 P-5, 2 P-4, 3 P-3, 1 P-2; and 3 GS) plus 6 new posts required overall (1 D-2, 1 P-5, 1 P-4, 1 P-3, 2 GS). For 2002-2003, only the D-2 post incurs additional regular budget resources; other resources to be met through ad hoc interim measures and residual extrabudgetary resources, with further requests to be made in the proposed programme budget for 2004-2005. Activities relate to programme 7 and sections 9, 2, and 27 of the programme budget.

SG statement (A/C.5/57/32) on PBI for Second Committee draft A/C.2/57/L.62. Additional requirements: Section 9: $95,500 for one D-2 Director post (head of the Financing for Development Office). Section 27D (Office of Central Support Services): $85,700 ($53,600 non-recurrent office alterations, furniture and fixtures; $32,100 recurrent rental of premises). Staff assessment of $22,000 is offset by income and excluded from new money. Total gross quoted in the statement is $203,200 including staff assessment; net new appropriation requested is $181,200. Implementation to begin at the start of 2003. Other posts (1 P-5, 1 P-4, 1 P-3, 2 GS) are indicated as required but to be requested in the 2004-2005 proposed programme budget, not part of this PBI. SG notes use of existing resources, redeployment within DESA, and residual extra-budgetary trust fund; unable to identify activities to terminate/curtail for absorption under sections 9 and 27D; therefore contingency fund appropriation would be required. Fifth Committee to address appropriations later in its consolidated statement.

Fifth Committee endorsed ACABQ recommendations and informed GA that, should draft resolution A/C.2/57/L.62 be adopted, there would be implications in the biennium 2002-2003 of up to $95,500 under section 9 (Economic and social affairs) and $85,700 under section 27 (Management and central support services). A related staff assessment of $22,000 under section 32 is fully offset by income section 1. Amounts are stated as 'up to' and are conditional on GA adoption of the draft resolution.

title: The right to development : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/62/L.49 : 20th report of the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions on the proposed programme budget for the biennium 2008-2009

stages.notes: ACABQ considered the SG’s statement A/C.5/62/18 on PBI for draft resolution A/C.3/62/L.49 (Right to development). SG identified additional requirements: $47,000 for activities under operative paras 1 and 39 (to be accommodated within Section 23) and $74,300 annually for the high-level task force sessions under operative para 3, to be accommodated within Section 2 ($63,300), Section 23 ($8,200) and Section 28E ($2,800). ACABQ recommends that the GA note the adoption would not entail additional resource requirements under Sections 2, 23 and 28E for 2008-2009; no contingency fund required. The $74,300 is an annual requirement; the $47,000 appears as a one-time activity within the biennium. Primary affected section: Section 23. No posts are mentioned.

This SG statement (A/C.5/62/18) covers programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/62/L.49 for the biennium 2008-2009. Under operative paras. 1 and 39, additional requirements of $47,000 (Section 23, Human rights) are for: (a) travel and DSA for two experts and one staff member to conduct two technical missions of five days each ($36,600); and (b) travel of the Chairperson of the Working Group to present a verbal update to the General Assembly ($10,400). The Secretariat proposes these be accommodated within Section 23 resources for 2008-2009; no additional appropriations are required.
With respect to operative para. 3, the SG previously indicated (A/61/530/Add.3) additional requirements of $74,300 per year for the two added working days of the high-level task force sessions (conference servicing and DSA) distributed as: Section 2 (GA and ECOSOC affairs and conference management): $63,300; Section 23 (Human rights): $8,200; Section 28E (Administration, Geneva): $2,800. These requirements will also be accommodated within the relevant sections of the proposed programme budget; no contingency fund use is needed per the conclusion in this statement.
Operative paras. 2 and 6 have existing provision in the proposed programme budget. Overall, the adoption of the draft resolution would not entail additional resources from the contingency fund. Primary affected sections: Section 23 (Human rights), with related conference servicing impacts in Section 2 and Section 28E.

Fifth Committee decided that adoption of draft resolution A/C.3/62/L.49 will not give rise to additional resource requirements under the cited sections of the 2008-2009 proposed programme budget. Secretary-General statement A/C.5/62/18 and ACABQ report A/62/7/Add.19 were considered.

title: Global efforts for the total elimination of racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance and the comprehensive implementation of and follow-up to the Durban Declaration and Programme of Action : programme budget implications of draft resolution A/C.3/62/L.65/Rev.1, as orally revised : statement / submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly

stages.notes: Preliminary programme budget implications for the biennium 2008-2009 related to draft resolution A/C.3/62/L.65/Rev.1 (Durban Review Conference preparations). Total estimated additional requirements: $7,061,600, with $266,900 to be absorbed within existing resources, leaving a preliminary net requirement of $6,794,700 to be charged against the contingency fund. By section (net): Section 2 GA and ECOSOC affairs and conference management $4,895,900; Section 23 Human rights $1,898,800; Section 28E Administration, Geneva $0 (fully absorbed). Line-item drivers include: (a) conference services for an open-ended intergovernmental working group ($1,632,500); (b) additional support staff to service the intersessional working group ($837,000); (c) conference services for regional preparatory conferences ($3,270,300); (d) travel for relevant special procedures and mechanisms to participate in Preparatory Committee and regional meetings ($825,100); (e) holding the first substantive session of the Preparatory Committee in Apr–May 2008 ($260,000); (f) staff travel to service regional conferences ($236,700). Of the $266,900 absorbed: $253,100 under Section 2 and $13,800 under Section 28E (two items of $6,900 each). Travel for LDC representatives and for NHRI/NGO representatives (approx. $804,100) is to be funded from extrabudgetary resources (not part of the regular budget ask). The Secretariat notes these are preliminary estimates subject to further consultations and to Member State decisions on the Conference structure and regional meetings. No specific posts or grades are established in this document; “additional support staff” is referenced without details. First-year (2008) versus second-year (2009) split is not provided; figures are biennial aggregates.
